shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: --- name: test-driven-development description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code --- # ...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
---
name: test-driven-development
description: Use when implementing any feature or bugfix, before writing implementation code
---

# Test-Driven Development (TDD)

## Overview

Write the test first. Watch it fail. Write minimal code to pass.

**Core principle:** If you didn't watch the test fail, you don't know if it tests the right thing.

**Violating the letter of the rules is violating the spirit of the rules.**

## When to Use

**Always:**
- New features
- Bug fixes
- Refactoring
- Behavior changes

**Exceptions (ask your human partner):**
- Throwaway prototypes
- Generated code
- Configuration files

Thinking "skip TDD just this once"? Stop. That's rationalization.

## The Iron Law

```
NO PRODUCTION CODE WITHOUT A FAILING TEST FIRST
```

Write code before the test? Delete it. Start over.

**No exceptions:**
- Don't keep it as "reference"
- Don't "adapt" it while writing tests
- Don't look at it
- Delete means delete

Implement fresh from tests. Period.

## Red-Green-Refactor

```dot
digraph tdd_cycle {
    rankdir=LR;
    red [label="RED\nWrite failing test", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ffcccc"];
    verify_red [label="Verify fails\ncorrectly", shape=diamond];
    green [label="GREEN\nMinimal code", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccffcc"];
    verify_green [label="Verify passes\nAll green", shape=diamond];
    refactor [label="REFACTOR\nClean up", shape=box, style=filled, fillcolor="#ccccff"];
    next [label="Next", shape=ellipse];

    red -> verify_red;
    verify_red -> green [label="yes"];
    verify_red -> red [label="wrong\nfailure"];
    green -> verify_green;
    verify_green -> refactor [label="yes"];
    verify_green -> green [label="no"];
    refactor -> verify_green [label="stay\ngreen"];
    verify_green -> next;
    next -> red;
}
```

### RED - Write Failing Test

Write one minimal test showing what should happen.

<Good>
```typescript
test('retries failed operations 3 times', async () => {
  let attempts = 0;
  const operation = () => {
    attempts++;
    if (attempts < 3) throw new Error('fail');
    return 'success';
  };

  const result = await retryOperation(operation);

  expect(result).toBe('success');
  expect(attempts).toBe(3);
});
```
Clear name, tests real behavior, one thing
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
test('retry works', async () => {
  const mock = jest.fn()
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockRejectedValueOnce(new Error())
    .mockResolvedValueOnce('success');
  await retryOperation(mock);
  expect(mock).toHaveBeenCalledTimes(3);
});
```
Vague name, tests mock not code
</Bad>

**Requirements:**
- One behavior
- Clear name
- Real code (no mocks unless unavoidable)

### Verify RED - Watch It Fail

**MANDATORY. Never skip.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test fails (not errors)
- Failure message is expected
- Fails because feature missing (not typos)

**Test passes?** You're testing existing behavior. Fix test.

**Test errors?** Fix error, re-run until it fails correctly.

### GREEN - Minimal Code

Write simplest code to pass the test.

<Good>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(fn: () => Promise<T>): Promise<T> {
  for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    try {
      return await fn();
    } catch (e) {
      if (i === 2) throw e;
    }
  }
  throw new Error('unreachable');
}
```
Just enough to pass
</Good>

<Bad>
```typescript
async function retryOperation<T>(
  fn: () => Promise<T>,
  options?: {
    maxRetries?: number;
    backoff?: 'linear' | 'exponential';
    onRetry?: (attempt: number) => void;
  }
): Promise<T> {
  // YAGNI
}
```
Over-engineered
</Bad>

Don't add features, refactor other code, or "improve" beyond the test.

### Verify GREEN - Watch It Pass

**MANDATORY.**

```bash
npm test path/to/test.test.ts
```

Confirm:
- Test passes
- Other tests still pass
- Output pristine (no errors, warnings)

**Test fails?** Fix code, not test.

**Other tests fail?** Fix now.

### REFACTOR - Clean Up

After green only:
- Remove duplication
- Improve names
- Extract helpers

Keep tests green. Don't add behavior.

### Repeat

Next failing test for next feature.

## Good Tests

| Quality | Good | Bad |
|---------|------|-----|
| **Minimal** | One thing. "and" in name? Split it. | `test('validates email and domain and whitespace')` |
| **Clear** | Name describes behavior | `test('test1')` |
| **Shows intent** | Demonstrates desired API | Obscures what code should do |

## Why Order Matters

**"I'll write tests after to verify it works"**

Tests written after code pass immediately. Passing immediately proves nothing:
- Might test wrong thing
- Might test implementation, not behavior
- Might miss edge cases you forgot
- You never saw it catch the bug

Test-first forces you to see the test fail, proving it actually tests something.

**"I already manually tested all the edge cases"**

Manual testing is ad-hoc. You think you tested everything but:
- No record of what you tested
- Can't re-run when code changes
- Easy to forget cases under pressure
- "It worked when I tried it" 鈮?comprehensive

Automated tests are systematic. They run the same way every time.

**"Deleting X hours of work is wasteful"**

Sunk cost fallacy. The time is already gone. Your choice now:
- Delete and rewrite with TDD (X more hours, high confidence)
- Keep it and add tests after (30 min, low confidence, likely bugs)

The "waste" is keeping code you can't trust. Working code without real tests is technical debt.

**"TDD is dogmatic, being pragmatic means adapting"**

TDD IS pragmatic:
- Finds bugs before commit (faster than debugging after)
- Prevents regressions (tests catch breaks immediately)
- Documents behavior (tests show how to use code)
- Enables refactoring (change freely, tests catch breaks)

"Pragmatic" shortcuts = debugging in production = slower.

**"Tests after achieve the same goals - it's spirit not ritual"**

No. Tests-after answer "What does this do?" Tests-first answer "What should this do?"

Tests-after are biased by your implementation. You test what you built, not what's required. You verify remembered edge cases, not discovered ones.

Tests-first force edge case discovery before implementing. Tests-after verify you remembered everything (you didn't).

30 minutes of tests after 鈮?TDD. You get coverage, lose proof tests work.

## Common Rationalizations

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Too simple to test" | Simple code breaks. Test takes 30 seconds. |
| "I'll test after" | Tests passing immediately prove nothing. |
| "Tests after achieve same goals" | Tests-after = "what does this do?" Tests-first = "what should this do?" |
| "Already manually tested" | Ad-hoc 鈮?systematic. No record, can't re-run. |
| "Deleting X hours is wasteful" | Sunk cost fallacy. Keeping unverified code is technical debt. |
| "Keep as reference, write tests first" | You'll adapt it. That's testing after. Delete means delete. |
| "Need to explore first" | Fine. Throw away exploration, start with TDD. |
| "Test hard = design unclear" | Listen to test. Hard to test = hard to use. |
| "TDD will slow me down" | TDD faster than debugging. Pragmatic = test-first. |
| "Manual test faster" | Manual doesn't prove edge cases. You'll re-test every change. |
| "Existing code has no tests" | You're improving it. Add tests for existing code. |

## Red Flags - STOP and Start Over

- Code before test
- Test after implementation
- Test passes immediately
- Can't explain why test failed
- Tests added "later"
- Rationalizing "just this once"
- "I already manually tested it"
- "Tests after achieve the same purpose"
- "It's about spirit not ritual"
- "Keep as reference" or "adapt existing code"
- "Already spent X hours, deleting is wasteful"
- "TDD is dogmatic, I'm being pragmatic"
- "This is different because..."

**All of these mean: Delete code. Start over with TDD.**

## Example: Bug Fix

**Bug:** Empty email accepted

**RED**
```typescript
test('rejects empty email', async () => {
  const result = await submitForm({ email: '' });
  expect(result.error).toBe('Email required');
});
```

**Verify RED**
```bash
$ npm test
FAIL: expected 'Email required', got undefined
```

**GREEN**
```typescript
function submitForm(data: FormData) {
  if (!data.email?.trim()) {
    return { error: 'Email required' };
  }
  // ...
}
```

**Verify GREEN**
```bash
$ npm test
PASS
```

**REFACTOR**
Extract validation for multiple fields if needed.

## Verification Checklist

Before marking work complete:

- [ ] Every new function/method has a test
- [ ] Watched each test fail before implementing
- [ ] Each test failed for expected reason (feature missing, not typo)
- [ ] Wrote minimal code to pass each test
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Output pristine (no errors, warnings)
- [ ] Tests use real code (mocks only if unavoidable)
- [ ] Edge cases and errors covered

Can't check all boxes? You skipped TDD. Start over.

## When Stuck

| Problem | Solution |
|---------|----------|
| Don't know how to test | Write wished-for API. Write assertion first. Ask your human partner. |
| Test too complicated | Design too complicated. Simplify interface. |
| Must mock everything | Code too coupled. Use dependency injection. |
| Test setup huge | Extract helpers. Still complex? Simplify design. |

## Debugging Integration

Bug found? Write failing test reproducing it. Follow TDD cycle. Test proves fix and prevents regression.

Never fix bugs without a test.

## Testing Anti-Patterns

When adding mocks or test utilities, read @testing-anti-patterns.md to avoid common pitfalls:
- Testing mock behavior instead of real behavior
- Adding test-only methods to production classes
- Mocking without understanding dependencies

## Final Rule

```
Production code 鈫?test exists and failed first
Otherwise 鈫?not TDD
```

No exceptions without your human partner's permission.
Tool Result #49 shell_command call_77vyRvzb4IosvbHWVwzrARja exit 0 05:15:37
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: from __future__ import annotations from dataclasses import dataclass, field import logging from pathlib import Path from typing impor...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
from __future__ import annotations

from dataclasses import dataclass, field
import logging
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional

from domain.video.config import VideoPipelineConfig
from domain.video.stages import (
    run_stage0_clean,
    run_stage1_transcode,
    run_stage2_silence,
    run_stage3_repair,
    run_stage4_asr,
    run_stage5_ai_text,
    run_stage6_5_resub,
    run_stage6_clip,
    run_stage7_correct,
    run_stage7_subtitle,
    run_stage8_cover,
    run_stage9_organize,
)
from domain.video.stages.base import StageResult
from domain.video.stages.utils import probe_duration_seconds


@dataclass
class PipelineResult:
    success: bool
    output_file: Optional[str] = None
    error_message: Optional[str] = None
    executed_stages: List[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    performance_summary: str = ""
    current_stage: int = 0
    last_stage: Optional[str] = None
    failed_stage: Optional[str] = None
    performance_report: Optional[Any] = None


class VideoPipeline:
    """Internal multi-stage video pipeline."""

    def __init__(
        self,
        config: VideoPipelineConfig,
        logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None,
        performance_tracker: Any = None,
        resource_monitor: Any = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.config = config
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("clonecut.domain.video.pipeline")
        self.performance_tracker = performance_tracker
        self.resource_monitor = resource_monitor
        self._stages: List[tuple[str, Callable[..., StageResult]]] = [
            ("stage0_clean", self._stage0),
            ("stage1_transcode", self._stage1),
            ("stage2_silence", self._stage2),
            ("stage3_repair", self._stage3),
            ("stage4_asr", self._stage4),
            ("stage5_ai_text", self._stage5),
            ("stage6_clip", self._stage6),
            ("stage6_5_resub", self._stage6_5),
            ("stage7_correct", self._stage7_correct),
            ("stage7_subtitle", self._stage7),
            ("stage8_cover", self._stage8),
            ("stage9_organize", self._stage9),
        ]

    def run(
        self,
        input_video: str,
        task_id: str,
        start_from_stage: int = 0,
        forum_post_info: Optional[dict] = None,
    ) -> PipelineResult:
        current = input_video
        executed: List[str] = []
        stage_start = max(0, int(start_from_stage))
        context: Dict[str, Any] = {"forum_post_info": _normalize_forum_post_info(forum_post_info)}
        last_stage_index = max(0, min(stage_start, len(self._stages) - 1 if self._stages else 0))
        last_stage_name: Optional[str] = None
        gpu_stage_indexes = {1, 2, 4, 6}
        tracking_started = False

        if self.performance_tracker is not None:
            try:
                path = Path(input_video)
                file_size_mb = 0.0
                if path.exists():
                    file_size_mb = path.stat().st_size / (1024 * 1024)
                duration_seconds = probe_duration_seconds(input_video)
                self.performance_tracker.start_video_processing(
                    task_id=task_id,
                    video_filename=path.name,
                    original_filename=path.name,
                    file_size_mb=file_size_mb,
                    video_duration_seconds=duration_seconds,
                )
                tracking_started = True
            except Exception as exc:
                self.logger.warning("start performance tracking failed: task=%s err=%s", task_id, exc)

        for idx, (stage_name, handler) in enumerate(self._stages):
            if idx < stage_start:
                continue

            if self.resource_monitor is not None:
                try:
                    self.resource_monitor.record_step_resource(
                        step_num=idx,
                        step_name=stage_name,
                        task_id=task_id,
                    )
                except Exception as exc:
                    self.logger.debug("resource snapshot failed: task=%s stage=%s err=%s", task_id, stage_name, exc)
            if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                try:
                    self.performance_tracker.start_stage(
                        task_id=task_id,
                        stage_name=stage_name,
                        gpu_accelerated=(idx in gpu_stage_indexes),
                    )
                except Exception:
                    pass
            try:
                result = handler(current, task_id=task_id, stage_name=stage_name, context=context)
            except Exception as exc:
                if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                    try:
                        self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                            task_id=task_id,
                            stage_name=stage_name,
                            success=False,
                            error_message=str(exc),
                        )
                        self.performance_tracker.add_error(task_id, str(exc))
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
                return PipelineResult(
                    success=False,
                    output_file=current,
                    error_message=f"{stage_name} exception: {exc}",
                    executed_stages=executed,
                    current_stage=idx,
                    last_stage=last_stage_name,
                    failed_stage=stage_name,
                    performance_report=perf_report,
                )

            executed.append(stage_name)
            last_stage_index = idx
            last_stage_name = stage_name
            if not result.success:
                if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                    try:
                        self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                            task_id=task_id,
                            stage_name=stage_name,
                            success=False,
                            error_message=str(result.error_message or ""),
                        )
                        self.performance_tracker.add_error(
                            task_id,
                            str(result.error_message or f"{stage_name} failed"),
                        )
                    except Exception:
                        pass
                perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
                return PipelineResult(
                    success=False,
                    output_file=current,
                    error_message=result.error_message or f"{stage_name} failed",
                    executed_stages=executed,
                    current_stage=idx,
                    last_stage=last_stage_name,
                    failed_stage=stage_name,
                    performance_report=perf_report,
                )
            if self.performance_tracker is not None:
                try:
                    self.performance_tracker.end_stage(
                        task_id=task_id,
                        stage_name=stage_name,
                        success=True,
                    )
                except Exception:
                    pass
            if result.output_file:
                current = result.output_file

        perf_report = self._finish_tracking(task_id, tracking_started)
        summary = f"internal pipeline executed stages={len(executed)}"
        if perf_report is not None:
            try:
                ratio = float(getattr(perf_report, "processing_speed_ratio", 0.0))
                if ratio > 0:
                    summary = f"internal pipeline executed stages={len(executed)} speed={ratio:.2f}x"
            except Exception:
                pass
        return PipelineResult(
            success=True,
            output_file=current,
            executed_stages=executed,
            performance_summary=summary,
            current_stage=last_stage_index,
            last_stage=last_stage_name,
            performance_report=perf_report,
        )

    def _finish_tracking(self, task_id: str, tracking_started: bool) -> Optional[Any]:
        if not tracking_started or self.performance_tracker is None:
            return None
        try:
            return self.performance_tracker.end_video_processing(task_id)
        except Exception:
            return None

    def _stage0(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage0_clean(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage1(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage1_transcode(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage2(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage2_silence(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage3(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage3_repair(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage4(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = context.get("forum_post_info") or {}
        hotwords = _normalize_hotwords(forum_info.get("hotwords"))
        speaker = str(forum_info.get("speaker", "")).strip()
        return run_stage4_asr(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
            hotwords=hotwords,
            speaker=speaker,
        )

    def _stage5(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage5_ai_text(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=context.get("forum_post_info") or {},
        )

    def _stage6(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage6_clip(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage6_5(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage6_5_resub(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage7_correct(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage7_correct(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage7(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        return run_stage7_subtitle(current, self.config.output_dir, task_id)

    def _stage8(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = _normalize_forum_post_info(context.get("forum_post_info"))
        context["forum_post_info"] = forum_info
        return run_stage8_cover(
            current,
            self.config.output_dir,
            task_id,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
        )

    def _stage9(self, current: str, task_id: str, stage_name: str, context: Dict[str, Any]) -> StageResult:
        self.logger.debug("pipeline stage start: %s task=%s", stage_name, task_id)
        forum_info = _normalize_forum_post_info(context.get("forum_post_info"))
        original_filename = str(forum_info.get("original_filename", "")).strip()
        return run_stage9_organize(
            current,
            output_dir=self.config.output_dir,
            task_id=task_id,
            keep_intermediate_files=self.config.keep_intermediate_files,
            forum_post_info=forum_info,
            original_filename=original_filename,
        )


def _normalize_forum_post_info(raw: Optional[dict]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    if not isinstance(raw, dict):
        return {}

    info: Dict[str, Any] = dict(raw)
    hotwords = _normalize_hotwords(info.get("hotwords"))
    info["hotwords"] = hotwords

    speaker = _pick_first_text(
        info,
        keys=("speaker", "speaker_id", "spk", "target_speaker"),
    )
    if speaker:
        info["speaker"] = speaker

    cover_titles = _normalize_cover_titles(info.get("cover_titles"))
    cover_up = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_up", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))
    cover_middle = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_middle", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))
    cover_down = _pick_first_text(info, keys=("cover_title_down", "灏侀潰鏍囬涓?))

    if not cover_up and len(cover_titles) >= 1:
        cover_up = cover_titles[0]
    if not cover_middle and len(cover_titles) >= 2:
        cover_middle = cover_titles[1]
    if not cover_down and len(cover_titles) >= 3:
        cover_down = cover_titles[2]

    compact_titles = [item for item in (cover_up, cover_middle, cover_down) if item]
    if compact_titles:
        cover_titles = compact_titles

    info["cover_title_up"] = cover_up
    info["cover_title_middle"] = cover_middle
    info["cover_title_down"] = cover_down
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_up
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_middle
    info["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] = cover_down
    info["cover_titles"] = cover_titles

    return info


def _normalize_cover_titles(value: Any) -> List[str]:
    if not isinstance(value, list):
        return []
    output: List[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for item in value:
        if isinstance(item, dict):
            text = str(item.get("text", "")).strip()
        else:
            text = str(item or "").strip()
        normalized = " ".join(text.split())
        if not normalized:
            continue
        if normalized in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(normalized)
        output.append(normalized)
        if len(output) >= 3:
            break
    return output


def _normalize_hotwords(value: Any) -> List[str]:
    candidates: List[str] = []
    if isinstance(value, list):
        candidates = [str(item).strip() for item in value]
    elif isinstance(value, str):
        candidates = [item.strip() for item in value.replace("\uFF0C", ",").split(",")]

    output: List[str] = []
    seen: set[str] = set()
    for item in candidates:
        text = str(item or "").strip()
        if not text:
            continue
        dedup_key = text.lower()
        if dedup_key in seen:
            continue
        seen.add(dedup_key)
        output.append(text)
    return output


def _pick_first_text(info: Dict[str, Any], keys: tuple[str, ...]) -> str:
    for key in keys:
        value = str(info.get(key, "")).strip()
        if value:
            return value
    return ""


__all__ = ["VideoPipeline", "VideoPipelineConfig", "PipelineResult"]

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

from domain.video import VideoPipeline, VideoPipelineConfig
from domain.video.stages.base import StageResult


def test_internal_pipeline_skeleton_runs(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")

    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    result = pipeline.run(input_video=str(source), task_id="video-1")

    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file is not None
    assert Path(result.output_file).exists()
    assert "stage0_clean" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage1_transcode" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage2_silence" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage3_repair" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage4_asr" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage5_ai_text" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage6_clip" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage6_5_resub" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage7_correct" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage7_subtitle" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage8_cover" in result.executed_stages
    assert "stage9_organize" in result.executed_stages
    assert result.current_stage == 11
    assert result.failed_stage is None
    srt = tmp_path / "out" / "video-1" / "_stages" / "video-1_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    manifest = tmp_path / "out" / "video-1" / "_stages" / "video-1_stage8_cover_manifest.json"
    assert srt.exists()
    assert manifest.exists()


def test_internal_pipeline_failure_exposes_breakpoint(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    missing = tmp_path / "missing.mp4"

    result = pipeline.run(input_video=str(missing), task_id="video-2", start_from_stage=1)
    assert not result.success
    assert result.failed_stage == "stage1_transcode"
    assert result.current_stage == 1


def test_pipeline_stage4_manifest_contains_forum_context(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "sidecar")
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    sidecar = tmp_path / "shared.srt"
    sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "pipeline context subtitle",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_SIDECAR_PATH", str(sidecar))

    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    result = pipeline.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        task_id="video-ctx",
        forum_post_info={"hotwords": ["topic-a", "topic-b"], "speaker": "spk-1"},
    )

    assert result.success
    manifest = tmp_path / "out" / "video-ctx" / "_stages" / "video-ctx_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"
    assert payload.get("hotwords") == ["topic-a", "topic-b"]
    assert payload.get("hotword_count") == 2
    assert payload.get("speaker") == "spk-1"


def test_pipeline_stage4_receives_explicit_hotwords(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage4(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None, hotwords=None, speaker=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        captured["hotwords"] = hotwords
        captured["speaker"] = speaker
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage4_asr", fake_stage4)
    result = pipeline._stage4(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage4-ctx",
        stage_name="stage4_asr",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "hotwords": ["topic-a", "topic-b", "topic-a"],
                "speaker": "spk-x",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    assert captured["hotwords"] == ["topic-a", "topic-b"]
    assert captured["speaker"] == "spk-x"


def test_pipeline_stage8_cover_titles_mapping_from_english_keys(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage8(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage8_cover", fake_stage8)
    result = pipeline._stage8(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage8-cover",
        stage_name="stage8_cover",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "cover_title_up": "line-up",
                "cover_title_middle": "line-middle",
                "cover_title_down": "line-down",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    mapped = captured["forum_post_info"]
    assert mapped["cover_titles"] == ["line-up", "line-middle", "line-down"]
    assert mapped["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] == "line-up"
    assert mapped["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] == "line-middle"
    assert mapped["灏侀潰鏍囬涓?] == "line-down"


def test_pipeline_stage5_receives_forum_post_info(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    cfg = VideoPipelineConfig(output_dir=str(tmp_path / "out"))
    pipeline = VideoPipeline(cfg)
    captured: dict = {}

    def fake_stage5(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *, forum_post_info=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=input_video)

    monkeypatch.setattr("domain.video.pipeline.run_stage5_ai_text", fake_stage5)
    result = pipeline._stage5(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        str(source),
        task_id="video-stage5-ctx",
        stage_name="stage5_ai_text",
        context={
            "forum_post_info": {
                "hotwords": ["hot-a", "hot-b"],
                "speaker": "spk-z",
            }
        },
    )

    assert result.success
    forwarded = captured["forum_post_info"]
    assert forwarded["hotwords"] == ["hot-a", "hot-b"]
    assert forwarded["speaker"] == "spk-z"

Output:
---
name: using-git-worktrees
description: Use when starting feature work that needs isolation from current workspace or before executing implementation plans - creates isolated git worktrees with smart directory selection and safety verification
---

# Using Git Worktrees

## Overview

Git worktrees create isolated workspaces sharing the same repository, allowing work on multiple branches simultaneously without switching.

**Core principle:** Systematic directory selection + safety verification = reliable isolation.

**Announce at start:** "I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace."

## Directory Selection Process

Follow this priority order:

### 1. Check Existing Directories

```bash
# Check in priority order
ls -d .worktrees 2>/dev/null     # Preferred (hidden)
ls -d worktrees 2>/dev/null      # Alternative
```

**If found:** Use that directory. If both exist, `.worktrees` wins.

### 2. Check CLAUDE.md

```bash
grep -i "worktree.*director" CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null
```

**If preference specified:** Use it without asking.

### 3. Ask User

If no directory exists and no CLAUDE.md preference:

```
No worktree directory found. Where should I create worktrees?

1. .worktrees/ (project-local, hidden)
2. ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/<project-name>/ (global location)

Which would you prefer?
```

## Safety Verification

### For Project-Local Directories (.worktrees or worktrees)

**MUST verify directory is ignored before creating worktree:**

```bash
# Check if directory is ignored (respects local, global, and system gitignore)
git check-ignore -q .worktrees 2>/dev/null || git check-ignore -q worktrees 2>/dev/null
```

**If NOT ignored:**

Per Jesse's rule "Fix broken things immediately":
1. Add appropriate line to .gitignore
2. Commit the change
3. Proceed with worktree creation

**Why critical:** Prevents accidentally committing worktree contents to repository.

### For Global Directory (~/.config/superpowers/worktrees)

No .gitignore verification needed - outside project entirely.

## Creation Steps

### 1. Detect Project Name

```bash
project=$(basename "$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)")
```

### 2. Create Worktree

```bash
# Determine full path
case $LOCATION in
  .worktrees|worktrees)
    path="$LOCATION/$BRANCH_NAME"
    ;;
  ~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/*)
    path="~/.config/superpowers/worktrees/$project/$BRANCH_NAME"
    ;;
esac

# Create worktree with new branch
git worktree add "$path" -b "$BRANCH_NAME"
cd "$path"
```

### 3. Run Project Setup

Auto-detect and run appropriate setup:

```bash
# Node.js
if [ -f package.json ]; then npm install; fi

# Rust
if [ -f Cargo.toml ]; then cargo build; fi

# Python
if [ -f requirements.txt ]; then pip install -r requirements.txt; fi
if [ -f pyproject.toml ]; then poetry install; fi

# Go
if [ -f go.mod ]; then go mod download; fi
```

### 4. Verify Clean Baseline

Run tests to ensure worktree starts clean:

```bash
# Examples - use project-appropriate command
npm test
cargo test
pytest
go test ./...
```

**If tests fail:** Report failures, ask whether to proceed or investigate.

**If tests pass:** Report ready.

### 5. Report Location

```
Worktree ready at <full-path>
Tests passing (<N> tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement <feature-name>
```

## Quick Reference

| Situation | Action |
|-----------|--------|
| `.worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| `worktrees/` exists | Use it (verify ignored) |
| Both exist | Use `.worktrees/` |
| Neither exists | Check CLAUDE.md 鈫?Ask user |
| Directory not ignored | Add to .gitignore + commit |
| Tests fail during baseline | Report failures + ask |
| No package.json/Cargo.toml | Skip dependency install |

## Common Mistakes

### Skipping ignore verification

- **Problem:** Worktree contents get tracked, pollute git status
- **Fix:** Always use `git check-ignore` before creating project-local worktree

### Assuming directory location

- **Problem:** Creates inconsistency, violates project conventions
- **Fix:** Follow priority: existing > CLAUDE.md > ask

### Proceeding with failing tests

- **Problem:** Can't distinguish new bugs from pre-existing issues
- **Fix:** Report failures, get explicit permission to proceed

### Hardcoding setup commands

- **Problem:** Breaks on projects using different tools
- **Fix:** Auto-detect from project files (package.json, etc.)

## Example Workflow

```
You: I'm using the using-git-worktrees skill to set up an isolated workspace.

[Check .worktrees/ - exists]
[Verify ignored - git check-ignore confirms .worktrees/ is ignored]
[Create worktree: git worktree add .worktrees/auth -b feature/auth]
[Run npm install]
[Run npm test - 47 passing]

Worktree ready at /Users/jesse/myproject/.worktrees/auth
Tests passing (47 tests, 0 failures)
Ready to implement auth feature
```

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Create worktree without verifying it's ignored (project-local)
- Skip baseline test verification
- Proceed with failing tests without asking
- Assume directory location when ambiguous
- Skip CLAUDE.md check

**Always:**
- Follow directory priority: existing > CLAUDE.md > ask
- Verify directory is ignored for project-local
- Auto-detect and run project setup
- Verify clean test baseline

## Integration

**Called by:**
- **brainstorming** (Phase 4) - REQUIRED when design is approved and implementation follows
- **subagent-driven-development** - REQUIRED before executing any tasks
- **executing-plans** - REQUIRED before executing any tasks
- Any skill needing isolated workspace

**Pairs with:**
- **finishing-a-development-branch** - REQUIRED for cleanup after work complete

Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
---
name: requesting-code-review
description: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements
---

# Requesting Code Review

Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent to catch issues before they cascade. The reviewer gets precisely crafted context for evaluation 鈥?never your session's history. This keeps the reviewer focused on the work product, not your thought process, and preserves your own context for continued work.

**Core principle:** Review early, review often.

## When to Request Review

**Mandatory:**
- After each task in subagent-driven development
- After completing major feature
- Before merge to main

**Optional but valuable:**
- When stuck (fresh perspective)
- Before refactoring (baseline check)
- After fixing complex bug

## How to Request

**1. Get git SHAs:**
```bash
BASE_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD~1)  # or origin/main
HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)
```

**2. Dispatch code-reviewer subagent:**

Use Task tool with superpowers:code-reviewer type, fill template at `code-reviewer.md`

**Placeholders:**
- `{WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED}` - What you just built
- `{PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS}` - What it should do
- `{BASE_SHA}` - Starting commit
- `{HEAD_SHA}` - Ending commit
- `{DESCRIPTION}` - Brief summary

**3. Act on feedback:**
- Fix Critical issues immediately
- Fix Important issues before proceeding
- Note Minor issues for later
- Push back if reviewer is wrong (with reasoning)

## Example

```
[Just completed Task 2: Add verification function]

You: Let me request code review before proceeding.

BASE_SHA=$(git log --oneline | grep "Task 1" | head -1 | awk '{print $1}')
HEAD_SHA=$(git rev-parse HEAD)

[Dispatch superpowers:code-reviewer subagent]
  WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: Verification and repair functions for conversation index
  PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task 2 from docs/superpowers/plans/deployment-plan.md
  BASE_SHA: a7981ec
  HEAD_SHA: 3df7661
  DESCRIPTION: Added verifyIndex() and repairIndex() with 4 issue types

[Subagent returns]:
  Strengths: Clean architecture, real tests
  Issues:
    Important: Missing progress indicators
    Minor: Magic number (100) for reporting interval
  Assessment: Ready to proceed

You: [Fix progress indicators]
[Continue to Task 3]
```

## Integration with Workflows

**Subagent-Driven Development:**
- Review after EACH task
- Catch issues before they compound
- Fix before moving to next task

**Executing Plans:**
- Review after each batch (3 tasks)
- Get feedback, apply, continue

**Ad-Hoc Development:**
- Review before merge
- Review when stuck

## Red Flags

**Never:**
- Skip review because "it's simple"
- Ignore Critical issues
- Proceed with unfixed Important issues
- Argue with valid technical feedback

**If reviewer wrong:**
- Push back with technical reasoning
- Show code/tests that prove it works
- Request clarification

See template at: requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md

# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Code Organization

    You reason best about code you can hold in context at once, and your edits are more
    reliable when files are focused. Keep this in mind:
    - Follow the file structure defined in the plan
    - Each file should have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface
    - If a file you're creating is growing beyond the plan's intent, stop and report
      it as DONE_WITH_CONCERNS 鈥?don't split files on your own without plan guidance
    - If an existing file you're modifying is already large or tangled, work carefully
      and note it as a concern in your report
    - In existing codebases, follow established patterns. Improve code you're touching
      the way a good developer would, but don't restructure things outside your task.

    ## When You're in Over Your Head

    It is always OK to stop and say "this is too hard for me." Bad work is worse than
    no work. You will not be penalized for escalating.

    **STOP and escalate when:**
    - The task requires architectural decisions with multiple valid approaches
    - You need to understand code beyond what was provided and can't find clarity
    - You feel uncertain about whether your approach is correct
    - The task involves restructuring existing code in ways the plan didn't anticipate
    - You've been reading file after file trying to understand the system without progress

    **How to escalate:** Report back with status BLOCKED or NEEDS_CONTEXT. Describe
    specifically what you're stuck on, what you've tried, and what kind of help you need.
    The controller can provide more context, re-dispatch with a more capable model,
    or break the task into smaller pieces.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - **Status:** DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT
    - What you implemented (or what you attempted, if blocked)
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns

    Use DONE_WITH_CONCERNS if you completed the work but have doubts about correctness.
    Use BLOCKED if you cannot complete the task. Use NEEDS_CONTEXT if you need
    information that wasn't provided. Never silently produce work you're unsure about.
```

Output:
# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - 鉁?Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - 鉂?Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```

# Code Quality Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a code quality reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementation is well-built (clean, tested, maintainable)

**Only dispatch after spec compliance review passes.**

```
Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer):
  Use template at requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md

  WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: [from implementer's report]
  PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task N from [plan-file]
  BASE_SHA: [commit before task]
  HEAD_SHA: [current commit]
  DESCRIPTION: [task summary]
```

**In addition to standard code quality concerns, the reviewer should check:**
- Does each file have one clear responsibility with a well-defined interface?
- Are units decomposed so they can be understood and tested independently?
- Is the implementation following the file structure from the plan?
- Did this implementation create new files that are already large, or significantly grow existing files? (Don't flag pre-existing file sizes 鈥?focus on what this change contributed.)

**Code reviewer returns:** Strengths, Issues (Critical/Important/Minor), Assessment

This is the narrowest unblocker and gives the fastest signal that the new path is real.\n\nFiles:\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/__init__.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/base.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/utils.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage0_clean.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage1_transcode.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage2_silence.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage3_repair.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage4_asr.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage5_ai_text.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage6_clip.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage6_5_resub.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage7_correct.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage7_subtitle.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage8_cover.py`\n- Create: `domain/video/stages/stage9_organize.py`\n- Modify: `domain/video/__init__.py`\n- Modify: `domain/video/pipeline.py`\n- Test: `tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py`\n\nStep 1: Freeze the exact stage import surface\n- The stage symbol list in `domain/video/pipeline.py` must match the files you create.\n- The tests reference the same names; do not invent a parallel naming scheme.\n\nStep 2: Write the failing import check\n- Baseline is already observed failing with:\n  - `ImportError: cannot import name 'VideoPipeline' from 'domain.video'`\n  - `tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py` fails at collection for the same reason.\n\nStep 3: Add the minimum shared stage contract\n- Create `domain/video/stages/base.py` with one small `StageResult` dataclass returned by every stage.\n- Create `domain/video/stages/utils.py` with `ensure_stage_dir(output_dir: str, task_id: str) -> Path` and `probe_duration_seconds(_input_video: str) -> float`.\n\nStep 4: Add stage modules as minimal pass-through implementations\n- For each stage module, start with the smallest deterministic implementation that preserves file outputs and stage names instead of business accuracy.\n- The goal in this task is importability and pipeline shape, not algorithm parity.\n- Use stage-specific output/manifest files where `tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py` requires them.\n\nStep 5: Re-export the new public entry\n- `domain/video/__init__.py` must export `VideoPipeline` and `VideoPipelineConfig`.\n\nStep 6: Run the first unblock verification\n- Use `D:\\clonecut\\.venv-p310\\Scripts\\python.exe`.\n- Verify:\n  1. `from domain.video import VideoPipeline, VideoPipelineConfig` prints both names.\n  2. `D:\\clonecut\\.venv-p310\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py -q` executes and should pass for this task.\n\nDo not commit anything yet.\n\n## Context\n\n- Work ONLY in this isolated worktree: `C:\\Users\\ai\\.config\\superpowers\\worktrees\\clonecut\\codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328`\n- The repo root `d:\\clonecut` is dirty; do not edit there.\n- You are not alone in the codebase. Do not revert edits you didn't make. Adjust to the existing code.\n- Current `domain/video/pipeline.py` already imports these stage symbols and uses `StageResult` + `probe_duration_seconds`.\n- Current `domain/video/__init__.py` is intentionally empty and causes import failure.\n- Existing failing test is already present in `tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py`; treat that as the RED state for TDD.\n\n## Before You Begin\n\nIf anything is unclear, ask now before starting.\n\n## Your Job\n\n1. Implement exactly this task.\n2. Keep implementations minimal and deterministic.\n3. Use the existing failing import/test as the RED step.\n4. Verify with the interpreter and pytest command above.\n5. Self-review before reporting.\n\n## File Ownership\n\nYou own only these paths for this task:\n- `domain/video/__init__.py`\n- `domain/video/pipeline.py`\n- `domain/video/stages/**`\n- `tests/unit/test_video_pipeline.py` only if you truly need a minimal test adjustment to reflect the approved plan.\n\n## Report Format\n\nReport:\n- Status: DONE | DONE_WITH_CONCERNS | BLOCKED | NEEDS_CONTEXT\n- What you implemented\n- What you tested and results\n- Files changed\n- Self-review findings\n- Any concerns"
}

Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.4 seconds
Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

from domain.video.stages import stage4_asr, stage5_ai_text


def test_stage4_prefers_sidecar_subtitle(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "sidecar")
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    sidecar = tmp_path / "input.srt"
    sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,500",
                "hello world",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:01,500 --> 00:00:03,000",
                "second line",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v1")
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    assert Path(str(result.output_file)).exists()

    srt = out_dir / "v1" / "_stages" / "v1_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    transcript = out_dir / "v1" / "_stages" / "v1_stage4_asr_transcript.txt"
    manifest = out_dir / "v1" / "_stages" / "v1_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    assert srt.exists()
    assert transcript.exists()
    assert manifest.exists()
    assert "hello world" in srt.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is False
    attempts = payload.get("attempts") or []
    assert attempts and attempts[0].get("backend") == "sidecar"


def test_stage4_auto_mode_can_prioritize_sidecar(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "auto")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_PREFER_SIDECAR", "true")
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    sidecar = tmp_path / "input.srt"
    sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "auto mode sidecar",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v_auto")
    assert result.success
    manifest = out_dir / "v_auto" / "_stages" / "v_auto_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    attempts = payload.get("attempts") or []
    assert attempts and attempts[0].get("backend") == "sidecar"
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"


def test_stage4_can_use_external_sidecar_path(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    shared_sidecar = tmp_path / "shared_total.srt"
    shared_sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:03,000",
                "shared line one",
                "shared line two",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "sidecar")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_SIDECAR_PATH", str(shared_sidecar))

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v_sidecar_path")
    assert result.success

    srt = out_dir / "v_sidecar_path" / "_stages" / "v_sidecar_path_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    saved = srt.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "shared line one" in saved
    assert "shared line two" in saved

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v_sidecar_path" / "_stages" / "v_sidecar_path_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"


def test_stage4_manifest_tracks_hotwords_and_speaker(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "sidecar")
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    sidecar = tmp_path / "input.srt"
    sidecar.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,500",
                "context line",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id="v_ctx",
        forum_post_info={"hotwords": ["alpha", "beta", "alpha"], "speaker": "spk-A"},
    )
    assert result.success

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v_ctx" / "_stages" / "v_ctx_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "sidecar_srt"
    assert payload.get("hotwords") == ["alpha", "beta"]
    assert payload.get("hotword_count") == 2
    assert payload.get("speaker") == "spk-A"


def test_stage4_fallback_to_timeline_when_no_backend_result(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "faster_whisper")
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        stage4_asr,
        "_resolve_segments",
        lambda **_: ([], "timeline_fallback", "test empty whisper", []),
    )

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v_timeline")
    assert result.success
    manifest_path = out_dir / "v_timeline" / "_stages" / "v_timeline_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "timeline_fallback"
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True


def test_stage4_strict_mode_rejects_timeline_fallback(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_ASR_BACKEND", "faster_whisper")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "true")
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        stage4_asr,
        "_resolve_segments",
        lambda **_: ([], "timeline_fallback", "test empty whisper", []),
    )

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage4_asr.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v_timeline_strict")
    assert result.success is False
    assert "strict equivalence" in str(result.error_message)

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v_timeline_strict" / "_stages" / "v_timeline_strict_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("strict_rejected") is True
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True


def test_stage5_rule_based_script_generation(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_MODEL", raising=False)

    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v2" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / "v2_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "build a clear workflow",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:02,000 --> 00:00:04,000",
                "validate every stage with tests",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage5_ai_text.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v2")
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    assert Path(str(result.output_file)).exists()

    script = stage_dir / "v2_stage5_ai_script.txt"
    manifest = stage_dir / "v2_stage5_ai_manifest.json"
    pointer = stage_dir / "v2_stage5_script_path.txt"
    assert script.exists()
    assert manifest.exists()
    assert pointer.exists()

    content = script.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
    assert "Script Draft" in content
    assert "Key Points:" in content
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "rule_based"


def test_stage5_merges_forum_keywords_when_enabled(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_MODEL", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_USE_FORUM_KEYWORDS", "true")

    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v5_forum_on" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / "v5_forum_on_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "generic transcript sentence",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage5_ai_text.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id="v5_forum_on",
        forum_post_info={"hotwords": ["璁哄潧璇岮", "璁哄潧璇岯", "璁哄潧璇岮"]},
    )
    assert result.success
    manifest = stage_dir / "v5_forum_on_stage5_ai_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("use_forum_keywords") is True
    assert payload.get("forum_keyword_count") == 2
    assert payload.get("keywords")[:2] == ["璁哄潧璇岮", "璁哄潧璇岯"]


def test_stage5_can_disable_forum_keywords(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_MODEL", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_USE_FORUM_KEYWORDS", "false")

    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v5_forum_off" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / "v5_forum_off_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "generic transcript sentence",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage5_ai_text.run(
        input_video=str(source),
        output_dir=str(out_dir),
        task_id="v5_forum_off",
        forum_post_info={"hotwords": ["璁哄潧璇岮", "璁哄潧璇岯"]},
    )
    assert result.success
    manifest = stage_dir / "v5_forum_off_stage5_ai_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("use_forum_keywords") is False
    assert payload.get("forum_keyword_count") == 0
    assert "璁哄潧璇岮" not in (payload.get("keywords") or [])


def test_stage5_uses_llm_factory_rule_provider(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_USE_FACTORY", "true")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_PROVIDER", "rule")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_MODEL", "rule-echo")

    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v5_factory_rule" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / "v5_factory_rule_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "factory rule provider test",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage5_ai_text.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v5_factory_rule")
    assert result.success
    manifest = stage_dir / "v5_factory_rule_stage5_ai_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "llm_factory:rule"
    assert payload.get("llm_use_factory") is True
    assert payload.get("llm_error") == ""


def test_stage5_factory_failure_falls_back_to_rule(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_USE_FACTORY", "true")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_PROVIDER", "openai")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_MODEL", "gpt-test")
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE5_LLM_API_KEY", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_LLM_API_KEY", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("OPENAI_API_KEY", raising=False)

    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")
    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v5_factory_fallback" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    srt = stage_dir / "v5_factory_fallback_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    srt.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:02,000",
                "factory fallback test",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage5_ai_text.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v5_factory_fallback")
    assert result.success
    manifest = stage_dir / "v5_factory_fallback_stage5_ai_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("engine") == "rule_based"
    assert payload.get("llm_use_factory") is True
    assert "api_key" in str(payload.get("llm_error", ""))

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import json
from pathlib import Path

from domain.video.stages import stage6_clip


def test_build_clip_ranges_respects_max_duration(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_PAD_BEFORE_SECONDS", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_PAD_AFTER_SECONDS", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_MERGE_GAP_SECONDS", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_MIN_CLIP_SECONDS", raising=False)
    monkeypatch.delenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_MAX_SEGMENTS", raising=False)

    subtitle = tmp_path / "sample.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "line a",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:03,000 --> 00:00:04,000",
                "line b",
                "",
                "3",
                "00:00:08,000 --> 00:00:09,000",
                "line c",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    ranges, detail = stage6_clip._build_clip_ranges(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        subtitle_path=subtitle,
        video_duration=12.0,
        max_duration=3,
    )
    assert ranges
    total = sum(max(0.0, end - start) for start, end in ranges)
    assert total <= 3.01
    assert "merged=" in detail


def test_stage6_trim_prefers_stage6_codec_env(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    target = tmp_path / "out.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"input-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_VIDEO_CODEC", "hevc_nvenc")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_CODEC", "libx264")

    captured: list[list[str]] = []

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured.append(list(args))
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    ok, error = stage6_clip._render_trim_seconds(source=source, target=target, target_seconds=3.0)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    assert ok is True
    assert error == ""
    assert len(captured) == 1
    assert captured[0][captured[0].index("-c:v") + 1] == "hevc_nvenc"


def test_stage6_trim_gpu_codec_failure_downgrades_to_libx264(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    target = tmp_path / "out.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"input-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_VIDEO_CODEC", "h264_nvenc")

    captured: list[list[str]] = []
    calls = {"count": 0}

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        captured.append(list(args))
        calls["count"] += 1
        if calls["count"] == 1:
            return False, "No NVENC capable devices found"
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)
    ok, error = stage6_clip._render_trim_seconds(source=source, target=target, target_seconds=3.0)  # type: ignore[attr-defined]

    assert ok is True
    assert error == ""
    codecs = [cmd[cmd.index("-c:v") + 1] for cmd in captured]
    assert codecs == ["h264_nvenc", "libx264"]


def test_build_clip_ranges_supports_speaker_filter(tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    subtitle = tmp_path / "sample.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "[spk-A] line a",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:02,000 --> 00:00:03,000",
                "[spk-B] line b",
                "",
                "3",
                "00:00:04,000 --> 00:00:05,000",
                "[spk-A] line c",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    ranges, detail = stage6_clip._build_clip_ranges(  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
        subtitle_path=subtitle,
        video_duration=8.0,
        max_duration=0,
        target_speaker="spk-A",
    )
    assert len(ranges) == 2
    assert "speaker_filter target=spk-A matched=2/3" in detail


def test_stage6_clip_fallback_when_ffmpeg_missing(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage6_clip.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v6")
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    output_path = Path(str(result.output_file))
    assert output_path.exists()
    assert output_path.name.endswith("stage6_clip_copy.mp4")

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v6" / "_stages" / "v6_stage6_clip_manifest.json"
    assert manifest_path.exists()
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "copy_fallback"


def test_stage6_clip_strict_mode_rejects_copy_fallback(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STRICT_EQUIVALENCE", "true")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: False)
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"fake-video")

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage6_clip.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v6_strict")
    assert result.success is False
    assert "strict equivalence" in str(result.error_message)

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v6_strict" / "_stages" / "v6_strict_stage6_clip_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "strict_rejected"
    assert payload.get("fallback_used") is True


def test_stage6_clip_can_trim_with_duration_guide_path(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"input-video")
    guide_video = tmp_path / "guide.mp4"
    guide_video.write_bytes(b"guide-video")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_DURATION_GUIDE_PATH", str(guide_video))
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        stage6_clip,
        "probe_duration_seconds",
        lambda path: 12.3 if str(path).endswith("guide.mp4") else 30.0,
    )

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        out = Path(args[-1])
        out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_bytes(b"trimmed-video")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    result = stage6_clip.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v6_guide")
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    output_path = Path(str(result.output_file))
    assert output_path.exists()
    assert output_path.read_bytes() == b"trimmed-video"

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v6_guide" / "_stages" / "v6_guide_stage6_clip_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "duration_guide_trim"


def test_stage6_clip_text_match(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"input-video")
    clip_text = tmp_path / "clip_text.txt"
    clip_text.write_text("alpha line#gamma line", encoding="utf-8")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_PATH", str(clip_text))
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_MATCH", "true")
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_STAGE6_CLIP_TEXT_MIN_SCORE", "0.4")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "probe_duration_seconds", lambda _: 10.0)

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        out = Path(args[-1])
        out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_bytes(b"segment-or-concat")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v6_native" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    subtitle = stage_dir / "v6_native_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "alpha line",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:03,000 --> 00:00:04,000",
                "beta line",
                "",
                "3",
                "00:00:06,000 --> 00:00:07,000",
                "gamma line",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage6_clip.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v6_native")
    assert result.success
    assert result.output_file
    output_path = Path(str(result.output_file))
    assert output_path.exists()
    assert output_path.read_bytes() == b"segment-or-concat"

    manifest_path = out_dir / "v6_native" / "_stages" / "v6_native_stage6_clip_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "subtitle_clip_text_concat"


def test_stage6_clip_uses_stage4_manifest_speaker_filter(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    source = tmp_path / "input.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"input-video")
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "ffmpeg_exists", lambda: True)
    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "probe_duration_seconds", lambda _: 8.0)

    def fake_run_ffmpeg(args, timeout_seconds=1800):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        out = Path(args[-1])
        out.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        out.write_bytes(b"segment-or-concat")
        return True, ""

    monkeypatch.setattr(stage6_clip, "run_ffmpeg", fake_run_ffmpeg)

    out_dir = tmp_path / "out"
    stage_dir = out_dir / "v6_spk" / "_stages"
    stage_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    subtitle = stage_dir / "v6_spk_stage4_asr_subtitles.srt"
    subtitle.write_text(
        "\n".join(
            [
                "1",
                "00:00:00,000 --> 00:00:01,000",
                "[spk-A] alpha line",
                "",
                "2",
                "00:00:03,000 --> 00:00:04,000",
                "[spk-B] beta line",
                "",
            ]
        ),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )
    stage4_manifest = stage_dir / "v6_spk_stage4_asr_manifest.json"
    stage4_manifest.write_text(
        json.dumps({"speaker": "spk-A"}, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
        encoding="utf-8",
    )

    result = stage6_clip.run(input_video=str(source), output_dir=str(out_dir), task_id="v6_spk")
    assert result.success

    manifest_path = stage_dir / "v6_spk_stage6_clip_manifest.json"
    payload = json.loads(manifest_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    assert payload.get("method") == "subtitle_concat"
    assert "speaker_filter target=spk-A matched=1/2" in str(payload.get("detail") or "")
