  "message": "You are re-reviewing the CURRENT CloneCut worktree after follow-up fixes from a prior code-quality review.\n\nWorktree root: C:\\Users\\ai\\.config\\superpowers\\worktrees\\clonecut\\codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328\n\nFocus on these files:\n- apps/worker/service.py\n- domain/video/stages/stage5_ai_text.py\n- tests/unit/test_worker_service.py\n- tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py\n- tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py\n\n## Prior review issues that were supposed to be fixed\n1. state.json misreported progress at collapsed legacy-step boundaries (especially stage7_correct / stage7_subtitle cases).\n2. Tests did not cover the exact boundary failures and lacked one stronger direct-path clip_text propagation test.\n3. Minor stale warm-runner terminology remained in logging / helper naming.\n\n## Requirements\n- state.json must report the greatest completed legacy step only.\n- Failed stages must not be recorded as completed.\n- last_completed_step must stay within legacy semantics and match the reported step name.\n- worker mainline remains on direct VideoPipeline path, tiny-input fallback remains intact.\n- clip_text preservation/materialization must still hold.\n\n## Fresh verification already run after the latest fixes\n- .\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py -q\n- .\\.venv\\Scripts\\python.exe -m pytest tests/unit/test_worker_service.py -k \"internal_pipeline_executes_stage1_to_stage8 or internal_pipeline_real_video_pipeline_preserves_user_clip_text_into_stage6 or internal_pipeline_context_keeps_only_forum_snapshot_and_clip_text or fast_fallback_for_tiny_placeholder_video or attach_video_render_manifest_collects_primary_subtitle_cover_and_state\" -q\n\n## Output format\n### Strengths\n### Issues\n#### Critical (Must Fix)\n#### Important (Should Fix)\n#### Minor (Nice to Have)\nFor each issue include file:line, what is wrong, why it matters, and how to fix if not obvious.\n### Recommendations\n### Assessment\nReady to merge? Yes/No/With fixes\nReasoning: 1-2 sentences\n\nIf there are no Critical/Important issues, say so explicitly."
}

wait_agent output={"status":{"019d3384-be24-7960-8954-17e4e2c05e72":{"completed":"### Strengths\n- The direct `VideoPipeline` path is wired in the right place, and the tiny-input migration fallba...
Show output payload 
{
  "status": {
    "019d3384-be24-7960-8954-17e4e2c05e72": {
      "completed": "### Strengths\n- The direct `VideoPipeline` path is wired in the right place, and the tiny-input migration fallback is still explicitly preserved in [apps/worker/service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py).\n- The new tests do meaningfully improve coverage intent: boundary-failure cases were added in [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), the stronger direct-path `clip_text` propagation test was added in [tests/unit/test_worker_service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_service.py), and the stage5 preservation/materialization checks in [tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py) are good. That stage test module passes locally.\n\n### Issues\n#### Critical (Must Fix)\n- [apps/worker/service.py:65](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py) and [apps/worker/service.py:1427](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py): `state.json` writing is currently broken. `_write_internal_pipeline_state()` reads `INTERNAL_PIPELINE_COMPLETION_BY_STAGE`, but the file only defines `INTERNAL_PIPELINE_COMPLETED_LEGACY_STEP_BY_STAGE`; worse, the defined mapping stores plain ints while the caller expects `(step, step_name)` tuples. What is wrong: every direct `VideoPipeline` run now crashes with `NameError` before returning, so the claimed fix for legacy-step boundary reporting never actually executes. Why it matters: this blocks the worker mainline entirely and invalidates the key contract you asked to verify, including “greatest completed legacy step only” and “failed stages must not be recorded as completed.” How to fix: replace this with one canonical mapping from stage name to `(legacy_step_number, reported_step_name)` and use it consistently when writing `state.json`; the collapsed cases need explicit entries, e.g. `stage7_correct -> (6, \"stage6_5_resub\")`, `stage7_subtitle -> (7, \"stage7_subtitle\")`, etc. After that, rerun the worker tests.\n\n#### Important (Should Fix)\n- No Important issues beyond the critical blocker above.\n\n#### Minor (Nice to Have)\n- [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:13](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:53](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), and [apps/worker/service.py:253](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py): some stale warm-runner terminology is still hanging around in helper/config naming (`runner_mode`, `internal_pipeline_runner`, `internal_pipeline_warmup_on_start`, `internal_pipeline_runner_mode`). What is wrong: the implementation is now direct `VideoPipeline`, but the test scaffolding and a few worker attributes still describe the removed warm-runner model. Why it matters: it keeps the mental model muddy and makes future failures/reviews harder to interpret. How to fix: rename or delete the leftover runner-oriented knobs/helpers so the code and tests describe the current architecture directly.\n\n### Recommendations\n- Fix the `state.json` mapping first; nothing else in this review is merge-safe until that is corrected.\n- Rerun the exact worker verification after the fix. In the current tree, my rerun of `tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py -q` failed with 7 failures, and the targeted `tests/unit/test_worker_service.py -k ...` command failed with 2 failures. `tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py -q` passed.\n\n### Assessment\nReady to merge? No\n\nReasoning: the current worktree still has a blocking regression in `apps/worker/service.py` that crashes the direct `VideoPipeline` path before it can write valid legacy-step state. Because of that, the main review target is not actually fixed, and the cited worker verification does not pass on the current tree."
    }
  },
  "timed_out": false
}

What is wrong: every direct VideoPipeline run now crashes with NameError before returning, so the claimed fix for legacy-step boundary reporting never actually executes. Why it matters: this blocks the worker mainline entirely and invalidates the key contract you asked to verify, including “greatest completed legacy step only” and “failed stages must not be recorded as completed.” How to fix: replace this with one canonical mapping from stage name to (legacy_step_number, reported_step_name) and use it consistently when writing state.json; the collapsed cases need explicit entries, e.g. stage7_correct -> (6, \"stage6_5_resub\"), stage7_subtitle -> (7, \"stage7_subtitle\"), etc. After that, rerun the worker tests.\n\n#### Important (Should Fix)\n- No Important issues beyond the critical blocker above.\n\n#### Minor (Nice to Have)\n- [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:13](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:53](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), and [apps/worker/service.py:253](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py): some stale warm-runner terminology is still hanging around in helper/config naming (runner_mode, internal_pipeline_runner, internal_pipeline_warmup_on_start, internal_pipeline_runner_mode). 我这边抓到 reviewer 指的具体点了：不是逻辑方向错了，而是我刚才抽公共映射时把常量名对歪了，导致 state.json 写入路径会在 reviewer 的视角直接炸掉。这个属于很明确的单点回归，我现在就修正并重新跑同一组测试。

class WorkerService:
    def __init__(self, config: Any, logger: Optional[logging.Logger] = None) -> None:
        self.config = config
        self.logger = logger or logging.getLogger("clonecut.worker.service")
        self.booted_at = time.time()
        self.redis_client = build_redis_client(
            host=str(getattr(config, "redis_host", "127.0.0.1")),
            port=int(getattr(config, "redis_port", 6379)),
            db=int(getattr(config, "redis_db", 0)),
            password=str(getattr(config, "redis_password", "")),
            logger=self.logger,

Show output payload 
{
  "status": {
    "019d3384-be24-7960-8954-17e4e2c05e72": {
      "completed": "### Strengths\n- The blocking `state.json` regression is fixed: the code now uses a single canonical mapping in [service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py#L65) and writes `last_completed_step` plus a legacy-consistent `last_completed_step_name`.\n- The failure path now respects legacy semantics. In [service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py#L1422), failed stages are resolved back to the last successful internal stage before mapping, which correctly handles the collapsed `stage7_correct` / `stage7_subtitle` boundary.\n- Test coverage in scope is now much stronger. The direct `clip_text` path, tiny-input fallback, context materialization, and exact boundary failures are all exercised in [test_worker_service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_service.py), [test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py), and [test_stage4_stage5.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py).\n- `clip_text` preservation/materialization still holds through both worker context setup and stage5 behavior in [service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py#L1509) and [stage5_ai_text.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/domain/video/stages/stage5_ai_text.py#L29).\n\n### Issues\nExplicitly: no Critical or Important issues remain in this review scope.\n\n#### Critical (Must Fix)\n- None.\n\n#### Important (Should Fix)\n- None.\n\n#### Minor (Nice to Have)\n- [apps/worker/service.py:253](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py#L253), [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:13](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py#L13), [tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py:53](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py#L53): some stale warm-runner terminology still remains in helper/config naming (`runner_mode`, `internal_pipeline_runner`, `internal_pipeline_warmup_on_start`, `internal_pipeline_runner_mode`). What is wrong: the implementation is now direct `VideoPipeline`, but some names still describe the removed warm-runner design. Why it matters: this is mostly readability and maintenance friction. How to fix: rename the leftover helper/config fields and test scaffolding to describe the direct pipeline path, or drop obsolete knobs entirely if they are no longer consumed.\n\n### Recommendations\n- The behavioral fixes in scope now look correct and aligned with the stated requirements.\n- If you want one final cleanup pass before merge, trim the remaining warm-runner naming leftovers so the codebase matches the new architecture more cleanly.\n- I also reran `tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py -q` in the current worktree and got `8 passed`.\n\n### Assessment\nReady to merge? Yes\n\nReasoning: the previous blocker is resolved, and I do not see any remaining Critical or Important issues in the reviewed scope. The only residual issue is minor naming cleanup around removed warm-runner terminology."
    }
  },
  "timed_out": false
}

assistant #1410 final_answer 08:25:10

现在的情况是：Task 3 这轮阻塞已经收住了。

我把 [apps/worker/service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/apps/worker/service.py) 和 [stage5_ai_text.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/domain/video/stages/stage5_ai_text.py) 这条链路修到了可交差状态：
clip_text 不会再被 stage5 覆盖，worker 主路径就是 direct VideoPipeline，state.json 的 legacy step 语义也修正了，collapsed stage 边界也补了测试。[test_worker_service.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_service.py)、[test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py)、[test_stage4_stage5.py](C:/Users/ai/.config/superpowers/worktrees/clonecut/codex-video-pipeline-startup-20260328/tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py) 都更新了。

验证结果是：
- tests/unit/test_stage4_stage5.py -> 13 passed
- tests/unit/test_worker_service.py 目标选择集 -> 5 passed
- tests/unit/test_worker_internal_pipeline_runner.py -> 8 passed

review 结果也已经过了：
- spec review：✅ Spec compliant
- code-quality review：没有 Critical / Important 剩余问题，只剩一个 Minor，是少量 warm-runner 命名还没清理干净，不影响行为

所以如果你问“现在到哪了”，答案就是：这轮 Task 3 修复已经可继续往下走了，还没有提交 commit。下一步可以选：
1. 我顺手把那个 Minor 命名清理掉
2. 直接继续计划里的 smoke test / 后续收尾

    "mode": "tokens",

Tool Result #1429 shell_command call_aG2OW9OhcFFDQQWL65AN8J97 exit 0 08:27:02
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.6 seconds Output: --- name: verification-before-completion description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing ...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.6 seconds
Output:
---
name: verification-before-completion
description: Use when about to claim work is complete, fixed, or passing, before committing or creating PRs - requires running verification commands and confirming output before making any success claims; evidence before assertions always
---

# Verification Before Completion

## Overview

Claiming work is complete without verification is dishonesty, not efficiency.

**Core principle:** Evidence before claims, always.

**Violating the letter of this rule is violating the spirit of this rule.**

## The Iron Law

```
NO COMPLETION CLAIMS WITHOUT FRESH VERIFICATION EVIDENCE
```

If you haven't run the verification command in this message, you cannot claim it passes.

## The Gate Function

```
BEFORE claiming any status or expressing satisfaction:

1. IDENTIFY: What command proves this claim?
2. RUN: Execute the FULL command (fresh, complete)
3. READ: Full output, check exit code, count failures
4. VERIFY: Does output confirm the claim?
   - If NO: State actual status with evidence
   - If YES: State claim WITH evidence
5. ONLY THEN: Make the claim

Skip any step = lying, not verifying
```

## Common Failures

| Claim | Requires | Not Sufficient |
|-------|----------|----------------|
| Tests pass | Test command output: 0 failures | Previous run, "should pass" |
| Linter clean | Linter output: 0 errors | Partial check, extrapolation |
| Build succeeds | Build command: exit 0 | Linter passing, logs look good |
| Bug fixed | Test original symptom: passes | Code changed, assumed fixed |
| Regression test works | Red-green cycle verified | Test passes once |
| Agent completed | VCS diff shows changes | Agent reports "success" |
| Requirements met | Line-by-line checklist | Tests passing |

## Red Flags - STOP

- Using "should", "probably", "seems to"
- Expressing satisfaction before verification ("Great!", "Perfect!", "Done!", etc.)
- About to commit/push/PR without verification
- Trusting agent success reports
- Relying on partial verification
- Thinking "just this once"
- Tired and wanting work over
- **ANY wording implying success without having run verification**

## Rationalization Prevention

| Excuse | Reality |
|--------|---------|
| "Should work now" | RUN the verification |
| "I'm confident" | Confidence 鈮?evidence |
| "Just this once" | No exceptions |
| "Linter passed" | Linter 鈮?compiler |
| "Agent said success" | Verify independently |
| "I'm tired" | Exhaustion 鈮?excuse |
| "Partial check is enough" | Partial proves nothing |
| "Different words so rule doesn't apply" | Spirit over letter |

## Key Patterns

**Tests:**
```
鉁?[Run test command] [See: 34/34 pass] "All tests pass"
鉂?"Should pass now" / "Looks correct"
```

**Regression tests (TDD Red-Green):**
```
鉁?Write 鈫?Run (pass) 鈫?Revert fix 鈫?Run (MUST FAIL) 鈫?Restore 鈫?Run (pass)
鉂?"I've written a regression test" (without red-green verification)
```

**Build:**
```
鉁?[Run build] [See: exit 0] "Build passes"
鉂?"Linter passed" (linter doesn't check compilation)
```

**Requirements:**
```
鉁?Re-read plan 鈫?Create checklist 鈫?Verify each 鈫?Report gaps or completion
鉂?"Tests pass, phase complete"
```

**Agent delegation:**
```
鉁?Agent reports success 鈫?Check VCS diff 鈫?Verify changes 鈫?Report actual state
鉂?Trust agent report
```

## Why This Matters

From 24 failure memories:
- your human partner said "I don't believe you" - trust broken
- Undefined functions shipped - would crash
- Missing requirements shipped - incomplete features
- Time wasted on false completion 鈫?redirect 鈫?rework
- Violates: "Honesty is a core value. If you lie, you'll be replaced."

## When To Apply

**ALWAYS before:**
- ANY variation of success/completion claims
- ANY expression of satisfaction
- ANY positive statement about work state
- Committing, PR creation, task completion
- Moving to next task
- Delegating to agents

**Rule applies to:**
- Exact phrases
- Paraphrases and synonyms
- Implications of success
- ANY communication suggesting completion/correctness

## The Bottom Line

**No shortcuts for verification.**

Run the command. Read the output. THEN claim the result.

This is non-negotiable.

+        if checkpoint:
+            task.stage_checkpoint = checkpoint
+        task.current_stage = int(getattr(pipeline_result, "current_stage", start_from_stage))

+            stage_name=checkpoint,
+            current_stage=task.current_stage,
+            failed_stage=failed_stage,
+            succeeded=succeeded,
+        )
+
+        if not succeeded:
+            error_message = str(getattr(pipeline_result, "error_message", "") or "").strip()
+            raise RuntimeError(error_message or "video pipeline failed")
+
+        return {
+            "success": True,
+            "request_type": task.task_type.value,
+            "output_path": output_path,

+                or f"video pipeline executed duration_ms={elapsed_ms}"
+            ),
+        }

+    def _should_use_internal_pipeline_migration_fallback(self, source: Path) -> bool:
+        threshold_raw = self._get_worker_cfg("internal_pipeline_migration_fallback_max_bytes", 0)

+            threshold = max(0, int(threshold_raw))
+        except Exception:
+            threshold = 0
+        if threshold <= 0:
+            return False
+        try:
+            return source.stat().st_size <= threshold
+        except Exception:
+            return False

+    def _write_internal_pipeline_state(
+        self,
+        *,
+        task_dir: Path,
+        output_path: str,
+        stage_name: str,
+        current_stage: int,
+        failed_stage: str = "",
+        succeeded: bool = True,
+    ) -> None:
+        last_successful_stage = self._resolve_internal_pipeline_last_successful_stage_name(
+            stage_name=stage_name,
+            failed_stage=failed_stage if not succeeded else "",
+            current_stage=current_stage,
+        )
+        completed_step, completed_stage_name = INTERNAL_PIPELINE_COMPLETION_BY_STAGE.get(
+            last_successful_stage,
+            (-1, ""),
+        )
+        payload = {
+            "last_completed_step": int(completed_step),
+            "last_completed_step_name": completed_stage_name,
+            "output_file": output_path,
+        }
+        state_path = task_dir / "state.json"
+        state_path.write_text(
+            json.dumps(payload, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2),
+            encoding="utf-8",
+        )
+
+    def _resolve_internal_pipeline_last_successful_stage_name(
+        self,
+        *,
+        stage_name: str,
+        failed_stage: str,
+        current_stage: int,
+    ) -> str:
+        stage_index = {name: idx for idx, name in enumerate(INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER)}
+        current_name = str(stage_name or "").strip()
+        failed_name = str(failed_stage or "").strip()
+
+        if failed_name:
+            if current_name and current_name != failed_name:
+                return current_name
+            failed_idx = stage_index.get(failed_name)
+            if failed_idx is not None:
+                if failed_idx == 0:
+                    return ""
+                return INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER[failed_idx - 1]
+            if int(current_stage) <= 0:
+                return ""
+            fallback_idx = min(len(INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER) - 1, int(current_stage) - 1)
+            return INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER[fallback_idx]
+
+        if current_name:
+            return current_name
+        fallback_idx = max(0, min(len(INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER) - 1, int(current_stage)))
+        return INTERNAL_PIPELINE_STAGE_ORDER[fallback_idx]
 
     def _prepare_internal_pipeline_context(self, task: UnifiedTask, task_dir: Path) -> Dict[str, Any]:
         payload = task.payload if isinstance(task.payload, dict) else {}
@@ -1522,51 +1506,12 @@ class WorkerService:
             metadata = next_metadata
             payload = next_payload
 
-        stage5_model_name = self._pick_first_text(
-            payload.get("stage5_model_name"),
-            metadata.get("stage5_model_name"),
-            payload.get("model_name"),
-            metadata.get("model_name"),
-        )
-        if stage5_model_name:
-            normalized_model, backend_model = self._normalize_internal_pipeline_model_name(stage5_model_name)
-            options["model"] = normalized_model
-            if backend_model:
-                options["openai_backend_model"] = backend_model
-
-        explicit_backend_model = self._pick_first_text(
-            payload.get("openai_backend_model"),
-            metadata.get("openai_backend_model"),
-            payload.get("openclaw_backend_model"),
-            metadata.get("openclaw_backend_model"),
-        )
-        if explicit_backend_model:
-            options["openai_backend_model"] = explicit_backend_model
-
-        file_encoding = self._pick_first_text(
-            payload.get("stage5_file_encoding"),
-            metadata.get("stage5_file_encoding"),
-         …3833 tokens truncated…ce_summary"])

-def test_run_internal_pipeline_falls_back_to_subprocess_when_warm_runner_fails(
+    assert result["executed_mode"] == "video_pipeline"
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("failed_stage", "current_stage", "expected_step", "expected_name"),
+    [
+        ("stage6_clip", 6, 5, "stage5_ai_text"),
+        ("stage6_5_resub", 7, 6, "stage6_clip"),
+        ("stage7_correct", 8, 6, "stage6_5_resub"),
+        ("stage7_subtitle", 9, 6, "stage6_5_resub"),
+        ("stage8_cover", 10, 7, "stage7_subtitle"),
+        ("stage0_clean", 0, -1, ""),
+    ],
+)
+def test_run_internal_pipeline_failure_reports_last_completed_legacy_step(
     monkeypatch,
     tmp_path,
+    failed_stage: str,
+    current_stage: int,
+    expected_step: int,
+    expected_name: str,
 ) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
-    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "warm_fallback.db"))

     service = WorkerService(_build_config(tmp_path))

-    source.write_bytes(b"video")
-    expected_output = Path(service.config.output_dir) / "video-task-1" / "fallback.mp4"
-    expected_output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
-    expected_output.write_bytes(b"done")
-
-    class FailingRunner:
-        def execute(self, *, payload, timeout_seconds):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
-            raise RuntimeError("warm failed")
-
-    service.internal_pipeline_runner = FailingRunner()

-    monkeypatch.setattr(service, "_resolve_internal_pipeline_output_path", lambda **_kwargs: expected_output)
-
-    class FakeCompletedProcess:
-        returncode = 0
-        stdout = ""
-        stderr = ""
-
-    subprocess_calls: list[dict] = []
-
-    def fake_run(cmd, **kwargs):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
-        subprocess_calls.append({"cmd": cmd, "kwargs": kwargs})
-        return FakeCompletedProcess()
-
-    monkeypatch.setattr(worker_service_module.subprocess, "run", fake_run)
-
-    result = service._run_internal_video_pipeline(_build_task(source), str(source))
+    source.write_bytes(b"video" * 64)
+
+    class FailingVideoPipeline:
+        def __init__(self, config, logger=None, performance_tracker=None, resource_monitor=None) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+            _ = config
+            _ = logger
+            _ = performance_tracker
+            _ = resource_monitor
+
+        def run(self, input_video, task_id, start_from_stage=0, forum_post_info=None):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+            _ = input_video
+            _ = task_id
+            _ = start_from_stage
+            _ = forum_post_info
+            return SimpleNamespace(
+                success=False,
+                output_file="",
+                error_message=f"{failed_stage} failed",
+                executed_stages=["stage1_transcode"],
+                performance_summary="failed",
+                current_stage=current_stage,
+                last_stage="",
+                failed_stage=failed_stage,
+                performance_report=None,
+            )
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(worker_service_module, "VideoPipeline", FailingVideoPipeline)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(
+        worker_service_module.subprocess,
+        "run",
+        lambda *args, **kwargs: (_ for _ in ()).throw(AssertionError("subprocess fallback should not run")),
+    )
 
-    assert len(subprocess_calls) == 1
-    assert subprocess_calls[0]["cmd"][0] == worker_service_module.sys.executable
-    assert result["output_path"] == str(expected_output)
-    assert "command=" in str(result["performance_summary"])
+    try:
+        service._run_internal_video_pipeline(_build_task(source), str(source))
+    except RuntimeError as exc:
+        assert f"{failed_stage} failed" in str(exc)
+    else:
+        raise AssertionError("expected video pipeline failure")
+
+    state_payload = json.loads(
+        (Path(service.config.output_dir) / "video-task-1" / "state.json").read_text(
+            encoding="utf-8"
+        )
+    )
+    assert state_payload["last_completed_step"] == expected_step
+    assert state_payload["last_completed_step_name"] == expected_name

+            captured["clip_text"] = clip_text_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")

+def test_internal_pipeline_real_video_pipeline_preserves_user_clip_text_into_stage6(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]

+    cfg = _build_config(tmp_path)
+    cfg.raw["worker"]["use_internal_pipeline"] = True
+    service = WorkerService(cfg)

+    source = tmp_path / "real-pipeline-source.mp4"
+    source.write_bytes(b"video-content" * 128)
+    task = UnifiedTask(
+        task_id="real-pipeline-clip-text-1",
+        task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
+        source="unit",
+        source_path=str(source),
+        payload={"clip_text": "user supplied clip text"},
+        metadata={"source": "unit", "forum_post_info": {"thread_id": "123"}},
+    )
+
+    final_output = Path(cfg.output_dir) / task.task_id / "final.mp4"
+
+    def passthrough_stage(input_video, output_dir, task_id, *args, **kwargs):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+        _ = output_dir
+        _ = task_id
+        _ = args
+        _ = kwargs
+        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(input_video))
+
+    def assert_clip_text_stage6(input_video, output_dir, task_id):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+        clip_text_path = Path(output_dir) / task_id / "clip_text.txt"
+        assert clip_text_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8") == "user supplied clip text"
+        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(input_video))
+
+    def finalize_stage9(
+        input_video,
+        *,
+        output_dir,
+        task_id,
+        keep_intermediate_files=False,
+        forum_post_info=None,
+        original_filename="",
+    ):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
+        _ = input_video
+        _ = keep_intermediate_files
+        _ = forum_post_info
+        _ = original_filename
+        final_output.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
+        final_output.write_bytes(b"final-video")
+        return StageResult(success=True, output_file=str(final_output))
+
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage0_clean", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage1_transcode", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage2_silence", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage3_repair", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage4_asr", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage6_clip", assert_clip_text_stage6)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage6_5_resub", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage7_correct", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage7_subtitle", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage8_cover", passthrough_stage)
+    monkeypatch.setattr(video_pipeline_module, "run_stage9_organize", finalize_stage9)
+
+    result = service._run_internal_video_pipeline(task, str(source))
+
+    assert result.get("output_path") == str(final_output)
+    assert result.get("executed_mode") == "video_pipeline"
+    assert (Path(cfg.output_dir) / task.task_id / "clip_text.txt").read_text(encoding="utf-8") == (
+        "user supplied clip text"
+    )

Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
        try:
            video_timeout = int(video_timeout_raw)
        except Exception:
            video_timeout = 7200
        self.video_adapter = ExternalVideoCommandAdapter(
            output_dir=self.config.output_dir,
            command_template=os.getenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TEMPLATE", video_cmd),
            timeout_seconds=int(
                os.getenv("CLONECUT_VIDEO_COMMAND_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", str(video_timeout))
            ),
            logger=self.logger,
        )
        self.internal_pipeline_enabled = self._bool_cfg(
            os.getenv("CLONECUT_USE_INTERNAL_PIPELINE"),
            bool(self._get_worker_cfg("use_internal_pipeline", False)),
        )
        self.internal_pipeline_runner_mode = "video_pipeline"
        self.internal_pipeline_pool_size = 0

        # --- telemetry ---
        telemetry_interval = float(self._get_worker_cfg("resource_monitor_interval_seconds", 5.0))
        report_dir = str(self._get_worker_cfg("performance_report_dir", "logs/performance_reports"))
        self.resource_monitor = ResourceMonitor(
            interval_seconds=telemetry_interval,
            disk_path=str(self.config.output_dir),
            logger=self.logger,
        )
        self.performance_tracker = PerformanceTracker(resource_monitor=self.resource_monitor)
        self.report_generator = ReportGenerator(report_dir=report_dir, logger=self.logger)

    def start(self) -> None:
        if any(thread.is_alive() for thread in self.worker_threads):
            return

        self.resource_monitor.start()
        recovered = self._recover_stuck_tasks()
        restored_retries = self._restore_retry_schedule()
        self.stop_event.clear()
        self.worker_threads = []
        self._spawn_workers("download", self.max_download_workers, self._download_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("process", self.max_process_workers, self._process_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("upload", self.max_upload_workers, self._upload_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("retry", 1, self._retry_scheduler_loop)
        self._spawn_workers("zombie", 1, self._zombie_cleaner_loop)

        self.logger.info(
            "worker adapters: tts_enabled=%s tts_engine=%s tts_http_ready=%s video_cmd_enabled=%s internal_pipeline=%s redis_registry=%s redis_queue=%s recovered=%s restored_retries=%s",
            self.tts_adapter_enabled,
            self.tts_engine_mode,
            self.tts_http_adapter.is_ready(),
            self.video_adapter.is_enabled(),
            self.internal_pipeline_enabled,
            self.registry.is_persistent(),
            self.use_redis_queue,
            recovered,
            restored_retries,
        )
        self.logger.info(
            "worker pools started: download=%s process=%s upload=%s retry_scheduler=%s zombie_timeout=%ss zombie_by_status=%s video_pipeline_path=%s migration_fallback_pool=%s",
            self.max_download_workers,
            self.max_process_workers,
            self.max_upload_workers,
            1,
            self.zombie_timeout_seconds,
            self.zombie_timeout_by_status_seconds,
            "direct",
            self.internal_pipeline_pool_size,
        )

    def stop(self) -> None:
Tool Result #1440 shell_command call_ow2mx5lx8BjMu8KRxaamORUL exit 0 08:27:21
shell_command output=Exit code: 0 Wall time: 0.5 seconds Output: import json from pathlib import Path from types import SimpleNamespace import apps.worker.service as worker_service_module import pyt...
Show output payload 
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:

import json
from pathlib import Path
from types import SimpleNamespace

import apps.worker.service as worker_service_module
import pytest
from apps.worker.service import WorkerService
from domain.task.models import TaskType, UnifiedTask


def _build_config(tmp_path, *, runner_mode: str = "subprocess") -> SimpleNamespace:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    return SimpleNamespace(
        redis_host="127.0.0.1",
        redis_port=6379,
        redis_db=0,
        redis_password="",
        redis_namespace="clonecut_worker_test:",
        input_dir=str(tmp_path / "input"),
        output_dir=str(tmp_path / "output"),
        log_dir=str(tmp_path / "logs"),
        raw={
            "queue": {
                "max_retries": 1,
                "retry_backoff_seconds": [1],
            },
            "worker": {
                "max_download_workers": 1,
                "max_process_workers": 1,
                "max_upload_workers": 1,
                "use_internal_pipeline": True,
                "internal_pipeline_runner": runner_mode,
                "internal_pipeline_pool_size": 1,
                "internal_pipeline_warmup_on_start": False,
                "video_command_template": "",
            },
        },
    )


def _build_task(source: Path) -> UnifiedTask:
    return UnifiedTask(
        task_id="video-task-1",
        task_type=TaskType.VIDEO,
        source="unit-test",
        source_path=str(source),
        payload={},
        metadata={},
    )


def test_worker_service_start_and_stop_skips_legacy_warm_runner_lifecycle(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "runner_lifecycle.db"))
    events: list[str] = []

    service = WorkerService(_build_config(tmp_path, runner_mode="warm_process"))
    service.resource_monitor = SimpleNamespace(
        start=lambda: events.append("resource_start"),
        stop=lambda: events.append("resource_stop"),
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(service, "_recover_stuck_tasks", lambda: 0)
    monkeypatch.setattr(service, "_restore_retry_schedule", lambda: 0)
    monkeypatch.setattr(service, "_spawn_workers", lambda *_args, **_kwargs: None)

    service.start()
    service.stop()

    assert events == [
        "resource_start",
        "resource_stop",
    ]


def test_run_internal_pipeline_ignores_legacy_warm_runner_and_uses_video_pipeline(monkeypatch, tmp_path) -> None:  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
    monkeypatch.setenv("CLONECUT_DB_PATH", str(tmp_path / "video_pipeline_direct.db"))
    service = WorkerService(_build_config(tmp_path))

    source = tmp_path / "source.mp4"
    source.write_bytes(b"video" * 64)
    expected_output = Path(service.config.output_dir) / "video-task-1" / "final.mp4"

Output:
  video_command_timeout_seconds: 7200
  # Upload storage backend. Current supported value: local
  upload_storage_backend: "local"
  # Forum reply during upload stage. Only applies to video tasks carrying forum thread context.
  forum_upload_enabled: true
  forum_reply_content: "Video processing completed"
  forum_settings_path: "config/forum_settings.yaml"
  # Upload-stage retry backoff (seconds), used only for upload failures.
  upload_retry_backoff_seconds: [1, 2, 4]
  # Built-in stage pipeline switch (stage1~8). Enabled by default after B1 pass.
  use_internal_pipeline: true
  # subprocess | warm_process
  internal_pipeline_runner: "warm_process"
  internal_pipeline_pool_size: 1
  internal_pipeline_warmup_on_start: true
  # Retry/Zombie background scheduler tuning.
  retry_check_interval_seconds: 1
  zombie_timeout_seconds: 7200
  zombie_timeout_by_status_seconds:
    pending: 7200
    downloading: 7200
    processing: 7200
    uploading: 7200
  zombie_check_interval_seconds: 300

docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:200:- [x] `POST /api/tasks`：支持 `source_url/source_path/post_url` 三类输入。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:202:- [x] `GET /api/tasks`：可返回任务列表/任务状态。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:212:- [x] `POST /api/tasks/submit`：可手动提交待分发任务。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:213:- [x] `GET /api/tasks/pending`：可查看待分发任务。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:220:- [x] 发布口径（2026-03-01）：上述旧路径不再对外承诺；新版统一使用 `POST /api/tasks`、`POST /api/worker/receive-task` 以及 monitor 侧 `/api/tasks/submit`、`/api/send-task-v2`。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:255:- worker 关键接口在 `apps/worker/main.py` 已提供：`POST /api/worker/receive-task`、`GET /api/status`、`GET /api/resources`、`GET /api/tasks`、`POST /api/tasks`。
docs\archive\轻量与监控节点替代规划.md:256:- monitor 关键接口在 `apps/monitor/main.py` 已提供：`/api/start-monitoring`、`/api/stop-monitoring`、`/api/cluster/status`、`/api/cluster-status`、`/api/send-task-v2`、`/api/tasks/submit`、`/api/tasks/pending`、`/api/dashboard/monitor`、`/api/send-task`、`/api/posts`。

Output:
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import copy
import json
import logging
import mimetypes
import os
from pathlib import Path
import shutil
from datetime import datetime
from functools import wraps
from typing import Any
import uuid

from flask import Flask, jsonify, request, send_file
from jinja2 import ChoiceLoader, FileSystemLoader
from werkzeug.utils import secure_filename

from apps.web.mock_data import (
    build_worker_resources,
    build_worker_status,
    build_worker_tasks,
)
from apps.web import STATIC_DIR, TEMPLATE_DIR, render_worker_dashboard
from apps.worker.service import WorkerService
from infra.config.loader import load_app_config
from infra.logging.setup import setup_logging


RESULT_SCHEMA_VERSION = "1.0"


def _parse_optional_bool(value: Any) -> bool | None:
    if value is None:
        return None
    if isinstance(value, bool):
        return value
    text = str(value).strip().lower()
    if not text:
        return None
    if text in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}:
        return True
    if text in {"0", "false", "no", "n", "off"}:
        return False
    return None


def _should_start_embedded_worker(cfg: Any) -> bool:
    env_value = _parse_optional_bool(os.getenv("CLONECUT_API_START_WORKER"))
    if env_value is not None:
        return env_value

    raw = getattr(cfg, "raw", None)
    if isinstance(raw, dict):
        api_cfg = raw.get("api", {}) or {}
        cfg_value = _parse_optional_bool(
            api_cfg.get("start_worker", api_cfg.get("start_embedded_worker"))
        )
        if cfg_value is not None:
            return cfg_value

    return False


def create_app(service: WorkerService) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(
        __name__,
        template_folder=str(TEMPLATE_DIR),
        static_folder=str(STATIC_DIR),
        static_url_path="/web/static",
    )
    if app.jinja_loader is not None:
        app.jinja_loader = ChoiceLoader([app.jinja_loader, FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATE_DIR))])

    _api_token: str = os.getenv("CLONECUT_API_TOKEN", "").strip()
    _web_mock_mode = os.getenv("CLONECUT_WEB_MOCK_MODE", "").strip().lower() == "true"
    _api_upload_root = Path(getattr(service.config, "input_dir", "input")) / "_api_uploads"
    _api_upload_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)

    def _mock_mode_from_request() -> bool:
        if _web_mock_mode:
            return True
        raw = request.args.get("mock", "").strip().lower()
        return raw in {"1", "true", "yes"}

    def _parse_optional_bool(value: Any) -> Any:
        if value is None:
            return None
        if isinstance(value, bool):
            return value
        text = str(value).strip().lower()
        if not text:
            return None
        if text in {"1", "true", "yes", "y", "on"}:
            return True
        if text in {"0", "false", "no", "n", "off"}:
            return False
        return None

    def _parse_json_object_field(field_name: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
        raw = str(request.form.get(field_name, "") or "").strip()
        if not raw:
            return {}
        try:
            parsed = json.loads(raw)
        except Exception as exc:
            raise ValueError(f"invalid {field_name}: {exc}") from exc
        if not isinstance(parsed, dict):
            raise ValueError(f"invalid {field_name}: expected JSON object")
        return dict(parsed)

    def _resolve_download_path(task: dict[str, Any]) -> Path | None:
        result = task.get("result") if isinstance(task.get("result"), dict) else {}
        artifacts = result.get("artifacts") if isinstance(result.get("artifacts"), list) else []
        for artifact in artifacts:
            if not isinstance(artifact, dict) or not artifact.get("is_primary"):
                continue
            candidate = str(artifact.get("path") or "").strip()
            if not candidate:
                continue
            path = Path(candidate)
            if path.exists() and path.is_file():
                return path
        upload = result.get("upload") if isinstance(result.get("upload"), dict) else {}
        candidates = [
            str(upload.get("uploaded_path") or "").strip(),
            str(task.get("output_path") or "").strip(),
            str(result.get("named_output_path") or "").strip(),
            str(result.get("output_path") or "").strip(),
        ]
        for candidate in candidates:
            if not candidate:
                continue
            path = Path(candidate)
            if path.exists() and path.is_file():
                return path
        return None

    def _build_status_url(task_id: str) -> str:
        return f"/api/tasks/{task_id}"

    def _build_download_url(task_id: str) -> str:
        return f"/api/tasks/{task_id}/download"

    def _build_artifacts_url(task_id: str) -> str:
        return f"/api/tasks/{task_id}/artifacts"

    def _build_artifact_download_url(task_id: str, artifact_id: str) -> str:
        return f"/api/tasks/{task_id}/artifacts/{artifact_id}/download"

    def _build_openclaw_result_url(task_id: str) -> str:
        return f"/api/tasks/{task_id}/openclaw-result"

    def _find_task_artifact(task: dict[str, Any], artifact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        result = task.get("result") if isinstance(task.get("result"), dict) else {}
        artifacts = result.get("artifacts") if isinstance(result.get("artifacts"), list) else []
        target = str(artifact_id or "").strip()
        if not target:
            return None
        for artifact in artifacts:
            if not isinstance(artifact, dict):
                continue
            if str(artifact.get("artifact_id") or "").strip() == target:
                return artifact
        return None

    def _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id: dict[str, dict[str, Any]], artifact_id: str) -> dict[str, Any] | None:
        target = str(artifact_id or "").strip()
        if not target:
            return None
        artifact = artifacts_by_id.get(target)
        if not isinstance(artifact, dict):
            return None
        return {
            "artifact_id": target,
            "role": artifact.get("role"),
            "kind": artifact.get("kind"),
            "filename": artifact.get("filename"),
            "path": artifact.get("path"),
            "relative_path": artifact.get("relative_path"),
            "download_url": artifact.get("download_url"),
            "is_primary": bool(artifact.get("is_primary")),
        }

    def _compact_json_like(value: Any) -> Any:
        if isinstance(value, dict):
            compacted: dict[str, Any] = {}
            for key, item in value.items():
                normalized = _compact_json_like(item)
                if normalized in (None, "", [], {}):
                    continue
                compacted[str(key)] = normalized
            return compacted
        if isinstance(value, list):
            compacted_list = [_compact_json_like(item) for item in value]
            return [item for item in compacted_list if item not in (None, "", [], {})]
        return value

    def _build_openclaw_result(task: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
        decorated = _decorate_task_for_api(task, include_openclaw_result=False)
        task_id = str(decorated.get("task_id") or "").strip()
        result = decorated.get("result") if isinstance(decorated.get("result"), dict) else {}
        render_manifest = result.get("render_manifest") if isinstance(result.get("render_manifest"), dict) else {}
        artifacts = result.get("artifacts") if isinstance(result.get("artifacts"), list) else []
        artifacts_by_id = {
            str(item.get("artifact_id") or "").strip(): item
            for item in artifacts
            if isinstance(item, dict) and str(item.get("artifact_id") or "").strip()
        }
        videos = render_manifest.get("videos") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("videos"), dict) else {}
        subtitles = render_manifest.get("subtitles") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("subtitles"), dict) else {}
        texts = render_manifest.get("texts") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("texts"), dict) else {}
        context = render_manifest.get("context") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("context"), dict) else {}
        cover = render_manifest.get("cover") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("cover"), dict) else {}
        logs = render_manifest.get("logs") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("logs"), dict) else {}
        pipeline_state = (
            render_manifest.get("pipeline_state") if isinstance(render_manifest.get("pipeline_state"), dict) else {}
        )

        openclaw_result = {
            "schema_version": RESULT_SCHEMA_VERSION,
            "task_id": task_id,
            "status": decorated.get("status"),
            "is_ready": str(decorated.get("status") or "").strip().lower() == "completed",
            "executed_mode": result.get("executed_mode"),
            "status_url": decorated.get("status_url"),
            "download_url": decorated.get("download_url"),
            "artifacts_url": decorated.get("artifacts_url"),
            "progress": {
                "current_stage": decorated.get("current_stage"),
                "stage_checkpoint": decorated.get("stage_checkpoint"),
                "last_completed_step": pipeline_state.get("last_completed_step"),
                "last_completed_step_name": pipeline_state.get("last_completed_step_name"),
            },
            "outputs": {
                "final_video": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(videos.get("primary") or "")),
                "subtitled_video": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(videos.get("subtitled") or "")),
                "no_subtitle_video": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(videos.get("no_subtitle") or "")),
                "coverless_video": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(videos.get("coverless") or "")),
                "alternate_video": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(videos.get("alternate") or "")),
                "subtitle_srt": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(subtitles.get("srt") or "")),
                "subtitle_ass": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(subtitles.get("ass") or "")),
                "script_text": _artifact_ref_by_id(
                    artifacts_by_id,
                    str(texts.get("script") or subtitles.get("script") or ""),
                ),
                "clip_text": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(texts.get("clip_text") or "")),
                "cover_image": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(cover.get("image") or "")),
                "forum_post_info": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(context.get("forum_post_info") or "")),
                "processing_log": _artifact_ref_by_id(artifacts_by_id, str(logs.get("processing") or "")),
            },
            "manifest": {
                "primary_artifact_id": render_manifest.get("primary_artifact_id"),
                "primary_download_url": render_manifest.get("primary_download_url"),
                "artifact_count": render_manifest.get("artifact_count"),
            },
        }
        return _compact_json_like(openclaw_result)

    def _decorate_task_for_api(task: dict[str, Any], include_openclaw_result: bool = False) -> dict[str, Any]:
        decorated = copy.deepcopy(task)
        task_id = str(decorated.get("task_id") or "").strip()
        if not task_id:
            return decorated

        decorated["status_url"] = _build_status_url(task_id)
        decorated["download_url"] = _build_download_url(task_id)
        decorated["artifacts_url"] = _build_artifacts_url(task_id)
        decorated["openclaw_result_url"] = _build_openclaw_result_url(task_id)

        result = decorated.get("result")
        if not isinstance(result, dict):
            if include_openclaw_result:
                decorated["openclaw_result"] = _build_openclaw_result(decorated)
            return decorated

        artifacts = result.get("artifacts")
        if isinstance(artifacts, list):
            for artifact in artifacts:
                if not isinstance(artifact, dict):
                    continue
                artifact_id = str(artifact.get("artifact_id") or "").strip()
                if artifact_id:
                    artifact["download_url"] = _build_artifact_download_url(task_id, artifact_id)

        render_manifest = result.get("render_manifest")
        if isinstance(render_manifest, dict):
            primary_artifact_id = str(render_manifest.get("primary_artifact_id") or "").strip()
            if primary_artifact_id:
                render_manifest["primary_download_url"] = _build_artifact_download_url(task_id, primary_artifact_id)
        if include_openclaw_result:
            decorated["openclaw_result"] = _build_openclaw_result(decorated)
        return decorated

    def _save_uploaded_video(task_id: str) -> tuple[Path, str, int]:
        uploaded = request.files.get("file")
        if uploaded is None:
            raise ValueError("missing file field")
        filename = secure_filename(str(uploaded.filename or "").strip()) or "video.mp4"
        suffix = Path(filename).suffix.lower()
        content_type = str(uploaded.content_type or "").strip().lower()
        allowed_suffixes = {".mp4", ".mov", ".mkv", ".avi", ".m4v", ".webm"}
        if suffix and suffix not in allowed_suffixes and not content_type.startswith("video/"):
            raise ValueError(f"unsupported video file type: {suffix or content_type or 'unknown'}")

        task_dir = _api_upload_root / task_id
        task_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        target = task_dir / filename
        uploaded.save(str(target))
        size_bytes = target.stat().st_size
        if size_bytes <= 0:
            target.unlink(missing_ok=True)
            raise ValueError("uploaded file is empty")
        return target, filename, size_bytes

    def _build_upload_task_payload(task_id: str, source_path: Path, original_filename: str) -> dict[str, Any]:
        payload = _parse_json_object_field("payload")
        metadata = _parse_json_object_field("metadata")
        forum_post_info = _parse_json_object_field("forum_post_info")

        for key in ("clip_text", "stage5_model_name", "stage5_file_encoding", "post_url"):
            value = str(request.form.get(key, "") or "").strip()
            if value:
                payload[key] = value
                metadata[key] = value

        stage5_use_preprocessor = _parse_optional_bool(request.form.get("stage5_use_preprocessor"))
        if stage5_use_preprocessor is not None:
            payload["stage5_use_preprocessor"] = stage5_use_preprocessor
            metadata["stage5_use_preprocessor"] = stage5_use_preprocessor

        if forum_post_info:
            payload["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info
            metadata["forum_post_info"] = forum_post_info

        metadata.setdefault("source", str(request.form.get("source", "openclaw_upload")).strip() or "openclaw_upload")
        metadata["original_filename"] = original_filename
        metadata["uploaded_via"] = "api_multipart"

        task_type = str(request.form.get("task_type", "video") or "video").strip() or "video"
        priority = str(request.form.get("priority", "normal") or "normal").strip() or "normal"

        task_payload: dict[str, Any] = {
            "task_id": task_id,
            "task_type": task_type,
            "priority": priority,
            "source_path": str(source_path),
            "payload": payload,
            "metadata": metadata,
        }

        post_url = str(request.form.get("post_url", "") or "").strip()
        if post_url:
            task_payload["post_url"] = post_url
        return task_payload

    def _require_token(f):  # type: ignore[no-untyped-def]
        @wraps(f)
        def decorated(*args: Any, **kwargs: Any) -> Any:
            if not _api_token:
                return f(*args, **kwargs)
            auth = request.headers.get("Authorization", "")
            token = auth.removeprefix("Bearer ").strip() if auth.startswith("Bearer ") else ""
            if not token:
                token = request.args.get("token", "").strip()
            if token != _api_token:
                return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "unauthorized"}), 401
            return f(*args, **kwargs)

        return decorated

    @app.get("/")
    def dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut API Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/web/worker")
    def worker_dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut API Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/health")
    def health() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"status": "ok", "service": "api"})

    @app.get("/api/ping")
    def ping() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "message": "clonecut api online"})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/status")
    def mock_worker_status() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, **build_worker_status()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/resources")
    def mock_worker_resources() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": build_worker_resources()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_tasks() -> Any:
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "10")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
        except ValueError:
            limit = 10
        tasks = build_worker_tasks(limit=limit)
        return jsonify({"success": True, "tasks": tasks, "total": len(tasks), "limit": limit, "offset": 0})

    @app.post("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_submit() -> Any:
        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        source_url = str(payload.get("source_url", "")).strip()
        if not source_url:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "missing source_url"}), 400
        task_id = f"mock-submit-{datetime.now().strftime('%H%M%S')}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id}), 201

    @app.get("/api/status")
    def status() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, **service.get_status()})

    @app.get("/api/queues")
    def queue_status() -> Any:
        payload = service.get_status()
        return jsonify(
            {
                "success": True,
                "queue_sizes": payload.get("queue_sizes", {}),
                "queues": payload.get("queues", {}),
                "task_status_counts": payload.get("task_status_counts", {}),
                "timestamp": payload.get("timestamp"),
            }
        )

    @app.get("/api/resources")
    def resources_current() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": service.get_resources()})

    @app.get("/api/resources/history")
    def resources_history() -> Any:
        try:
            minutes = max(1, min(1440, int(request.args.get("minutes", "60"))))
        except ValueError:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "invalid minutes param"}), 400
        return jsonify(
            {"success": True, "minutes": minutes, "history": service.get_resources_history(minutes=minutes)}
        )

    @app.get("/api/tasks")
    def list_tasks() -> Any:
        status = request.args.get("status")
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "20")
        offset_raw = request.args.get("offset", "0")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
            offset = max(0, int(offset_raw))
        except ValueError:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "invalid pagination params"}), 400

        try:
            payload = service.list_tasks(status=status, limit=limit, offset=offset)
        except ValueError as exc:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": str(exc)}), 400
        tasks = payload.get("tasks") if isinstance(payload.get("tasks"), list) else []
        payload["tasks"] = [_decorate_task_for_api(task) for task in tasks if isinstance(task, dict)]
        return jsonify({"success": True, **payload})

    @app.post("/api/tasks")
    @_require_token
    def create_task() -> Any:
        data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        ok, task_id, error = service.submit(data)
        if not ok:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error or "invalid task payload"}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id}), 201

    @app.post("/api/tasks/upload")
    @app.post("/api/tasks/upload-video")
    @_require_token
    def upload_video_task() -> Any:
        requested_task_id = str(request.form.get("task_id", "") or "").strip()
        task_id = requested_task_id or str(uuid.uuid4())
        stored_path: Path | None = None
        stored_dir: Path | None = None
        try:
            stored_path, filename, size_bytes = _save_uploaded_video(task_id=task_id)
            stored_dir = stored_path.parent
            submit_payload = _build_upload_task_payload(
                task_id=task_id,
                source_path=stored_path,
                original_filename=filename,
            )
            ok, resolved_task_id, error = service.submit(submit_payload)
            if not ok:
                raise ValueError(error or "invalid task payload")
        except ValueError as exc:
            if stored_dir is not None and stored_dir.exists():
                shutil.rmtree(stored_dir, ignore_errors=True)
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": str(exc)}), 400
        except Exception as exc:
            if stored_dir is not None and stored_dir.exists():
                shutil.rmtree(stored_dir, ignore_errors=True)
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": f"upload task failed: {exc}"}), 500

        return (
            jsonify(
                {
                    "success": True,
                    "task_id": resolved_task_id,
                    "filename": filename,
                    "stored_path": str(stored_path),
                    "file_size_bytes": size_bytes,
                    "status_url": f"/api/tasks/{resolved_task_id}",
                    "download_url": f"/api/tasks/{resolved_task_id}/download",
                    "artifacts_url": f"/api/tasks/{resolved_task_id}/artifacts",
                    "openclaw_result_url": f"/api/tasks/{resolved_task_id}/openclaw-result",
                }
            ),
            201,
        )

    @app.get("/api/tasks/<task_id>")
    def task_detail(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task": _decorate_task_for_api(task, include_openclaw_result=True)})

    @app.get("/api/tasks/<task_id>/artifacts")
    def task_artifacts(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        decorated = _decorate_task_for_api(task)
        result = decorated.get("result") if isinstance(decorated.get("result"), dict) else {}
        artifacts = result.get("artifacts") if isinstance(result.get("artifacts"), list) else []
        render_manifest = result.get("render_manifest") if isinstance(result.get("render_manifest"), dict) else {}
        return jsonify(
            {
                "success": True,
                "task_id": task_id,
                "artifacts": artifacts,
                "render_manifest": render_manifest,
                "download_url": _build_download_url(task_id),
            }
        )

    @app.get("/api/tasks/<task_id>/openclaw-result")
    def task_openclaw_result(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        return jsonify(
            {
                "success": True,
                "task_id": task_id,
                "openclaw_result": _build_openclaw_result(task),
            }
        )

    @app.get("/api/tasks/<task_id>/download")
    @_require_token
    def task_download(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        status = str(task.get("status") or "").strip().lower()
        if status not in {"completed", "uploading", "processing"}:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": f"task output not ready: status={status or 'unknown'}"}), 409

        download_path = _resolve_download_path(task)
        if download_path is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "downloadable output not found"}), 404

        return send_file(
            download_path,
            as_attachment=True,
            download_name=download_path.name,
            mimetype="video/mp4",
            conditional=True,
        )

    @app.get("/api/tasks/<task_id>/artifacts/<artifact_id>/download")
    @_require_token
    def task_artifact_download(task_id: str, artifact_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        artifact = _find_task_artifact(task, artifact_id)
        if artifact is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "artifact not found"}), 404

        artifact_path = Path(str(artifact.get("path") or "").strip())
        if not artifact_path.exists() or not artifact_path.is_file():
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "artifact file not found"}), 404

        guessed_mime, _ = mimetypes.guess_type(artifact_path.name)
        mime_type = str(artifact.get("mime_type") or guessed_mime or "application/octet-stream")
        return send_file(
            artifact_path,
            as_attachment=True,
            download_name=artifact_path.name,
            mimetype=mime_type,
            conditional=True,
        )

    @app.post("/api/tasks/<task_id>/cancel")
    @_require_token
    def cancel_task(task_id: str) -> Any:
        ok, error = service.cancel_task(task_id)
        if not ok:
            code = 404 if error == "task not found" else 409
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error}), code
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id})

    @app.post("/api/tasks/<task_id>/retry")
    @_require_token
    def retry_task(task_id: str) -> Any:
        ok, error = service.retry_task(task_id)
        if not ok:
            code = 404 if error == "task not found" else 409
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error}), code
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id})

    return app


def _run_http(app: Flask, host: str, port: int, logger: logging.Logger) -> None:
    try:
        from waitress import serve

        logger.info("serving with waitress on %s:%s", host, port)
        serve(app, host=host, port=port)
    except Exception:
        logger.info("waitress unavailable, fallback to flask builtin server on %s:%s", host, port)
        app.run(host=host, port=port, debug=False, use_reloader=False)


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="CloneCut API service")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=None, help="override service port")
    parser.add_argument("--host", type=str, default=None, help="override service host")
    parser.add_argument("--config", type=str, default="config/settings.yaml", help="config path")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    cfg = load_app_config(args.config)
    cfg.role = "api"
    cfg.port = args.port or cfg.port
    cfg.host = args.host or cfg.host
    if cfg.redis_namespace == "clonecut_monitor:":
        cfg.redis_namespace = "clonecut_worker:"

    setup_logging(cfg.log_dir, cfg.role)
    logger = logging.getLogger("apps.api")
    logger.info("api booting env=%s host=%s port=%s", cfg.env, cfg.host, cfg.port)

    service = WorkerService(config=cfg, logger=logger)
    embedded_worker_started = _should_start_embedded_worker(cfg)
    if embedded_worker_started:
        logger.info("api embedded worker enabled")
        service.start()
    else:
        logger.info("api embedded worker disabled; expecting dedicated worker service")
    app = create_app(service)
    try:
        _run_http(app, cfg.host, cfg.port, logger)
    finally:
        if embedded_worker_started:
            service.stop()
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import logging
import os
from typing import Any
import uuid
from datetime import datetime

from flask import Flask, jsonify, request
from jinja2 import ChoiceLoader, FileSystemLoader

from apps.web.mock_data import (
    build_worker_resources,
    build_worker_status,
    build_worker_tasks,
)
from apps.web import STATIC_DIR, TEMPLATE_DIR, render_worker_dashboard
from apps.worker.service import WorkerService
from infra.config.loader import load_app_config
from infra.logging.setup import setup_logging


def create_app(service: WorkerService) -> Flask:
    app = Flask(
        __name__,
        template_folder=str(TEMPLATE_DIR),
        static_folder=str(STATIC_DIR),
        static_url_path="/web/static",
    )
    if app.jinja_loader is not None:
        app.jinja_loader = ChoiceLoader([app.jinja_loader, FileSystemLoader(str(TEMPLATE_DIR))])
    _web_mock_mode = os.getenv("CLONECUT_WEB_MOCK_MODE", "").strip().lower() == "true"

    def _mock_mode_from_request() -> bool:
        if _web_mock_mode:
            return True
        raw = request.args.get("mock", "").strip().lower()
        return raw in {"1", "true", "yes"}

    @app.get("/")
    def dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut Worker Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api/worker",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/web/worker")
    def worker_dashboard() -> Any:
        return render_worker_dashboard(
            {
                "page_title": "CloneCut Worker Dashboard",
                "dashboard_mode": "worker",
                "api_base": "/api/worker",
                "mock_mode": "true" if _mock_mode_from_request() else "false",
            }
        )

    @app.get("/health")
    def health() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"status": "ok"})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/status")
    def mock_worker_status() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, **build_worker_status()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/resources")
    def mock_worker_resources() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": build_worker_resources()})

    @app.get("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_tasks() -> Any:
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "10")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
        except ValueError:
            limit = 10
        tasks = build_worker_tasks(limit=limit)
        return jsonify({"success": True, "tasks": tasks, "total": len(tasks), "limit": limit, "offset": 0})

    @app.post("/web/mock/worker/tasks")
    def mock_worker_submit() -> Any:
        payload = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        source_url = str(payload.get("source_url", "")).strip()
        if not source_url:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "missing source_url"}), 400
        task_id = f"mock-submit-{datetime.now().strftime('%H%M%S')}-{uuid.uuid4().hex[:6]}"
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id}), 201

    @app.post("/api/worker/receive-task")
    def receive_task() -> Any:
        data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        ok, task_id, error = service.submit(data)
        if not ok:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error or "invalid task payload"}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id, "message": "task queued"})

    def _list_tasks_impl() -> Any:
        status = request.args.get("status")
        limit_raw = request.args.get("limit", "20")
        offset_raw = request.args.get("offset", "0")
        try:
            limit = max(1, min(200, int(limit_raw)))
            offset = max(0, int(offset_raw))
        except ValueError:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "invalid pagination params"}), 400

        try:
            payload = service.list_tasks(status=status, limit=limit, offset=offset)
        except ValueError as exc:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": str(exc)}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, **payload})

    def _create_task_impl() -> Any:
        data = request.get_json(silent=True) or {}
        ok, task_id, error = service.submit(data)
        if not ok:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": error or "invalid task payload"}), 400
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task_id": task_id, "message": "task queued"}), 201

    @app.get("/api/worker/status")
    def worker_status() -> Any:
        return jsonify(service.get_status())

    @app.get("/api/status")
    def status_compat() -> Any:
        return jsonify(service.get_status())

    @app.get("/api/worker/resources")
    def worker_resources() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": service.get_resources()})

    @app.get("/api/resources")
    def resources_compat() -> Any:
        return jsonify({"success": True, "resources": service.get_resources()})

    @app.get("/api/worker/tasks")
    def worker_tasks() -> Any:
        return _list_tasks_impl()

    @app.get("/api/tasks")
    def tasks_compat() -> Any:
        return _list_tasks_impl()

    @app.post("/api/worker/tasks")
    def worker_create_task() -> Any:
        return _create_task_impl()

    @app.post("/api/tasks")
    def create_task_compat() -> Any:
        return _create_task_impl()

    @app.get("/api/worker/task/<task_id>")
    def worker_task(task_id: str) -> Any:
        task = service.get_task(task_id)
        if task is None:
            return jsonify({"success": False, "error": "task not found"}), 404
        return jsonify({"success": True, "task": task})

    return app


def _run_http(app: Flask, host: str, port: int, logger: logging.Logger) -> None:
    try:
        from waitress import serve

        logger.info("serving with waitress on %s:%s", host, port)
        serve(app, host=host, port=port)
    except Exception:
        logger.info("waitress unavailable, fallback to flask builtin server on %s:%s", host, port)
        app.run(host=host, port=port, debug=False, use_reloader=False)


def main() -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="CloneCut worker service")
    parser.add_argument("--port", type=int, default=None, help="override service port")
    parser.add_argument("--host", type=str, default=None, help="override service host")
    parser.add_argument("--config", type=str, default="config/settings.yaml", help="config path")
    args = parser.parse_args()

    cfg = load_app_config(args.config)
    cfg.role = "worker"
    if args.port:
        cfg.port = args.port
    if args.host:
        cfg.host = args.host
    if cfg.redis_namespace == "clonecut_monitor:":
        cfg.redis_namespace = "clonecut_worker:"

    setup_logging(cfg.log_dir, cfg.role)
    logger = logging.getLogger("apps.worker")
    logger.info("worker booting")
    logger.info("env=%s role=%s host=%s port=%s", cfg.env, cfg.role, cfg.host, cfg.port)
    logger.info(
        "redis=%s:%s db=%s ns=%s",
        cfg.redis_host,
        cfg.redis_port,
        cfg.redis_db,
        cfg.redis_namespace,
    )

    service = WorkerService(config=cfg, logger=logger)
    service.start()
    app = create_app(service)

    _run_http(app=app, host=cfg.host, port=cfg.port, logger=logger)
    return 0


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())
